notes: Mexico requires NOM-015 compliance; CE marking not accepted as equivalent; Spanish language mandatory; market surveillance via PROFECO

key_requirements: Safety requirements: mechanical strength; flammability; chemical content; small parts test; age categories; references ISO 8124
notes: NOM-252 covers safety requirements aligned with ISO 8124; mandatory certification for toys sold in Mexico

enforcement_body: INMETRO (Instituto Nacional de Metrologia Qualidade e Tecnologia)
key_requirements: Compulsory certification (OCP — Organismo de Certificação de Produto); ABNT NBR NM 300 series (ISO 8124 equivalent); small parts test; heavy metal migration; flammability; age labeling in Portuguese; INMETRO seal mandatory
notes: Brazil requires mandatory INMETRO certification via accredited OCP; cannot sell toys without INMETRO seal; testing to ABNT NBR NM 300 standards

regulation_name: ABNT NBR NM 300 series — Segurança de brinquedos
key_requirements: Mechanical and physical safety (NM 300-1); flammability (NM 300-2); chemical migration (NM 300-3); small parts criterion; age grading; labeling in Portuguese
notes: Technical standards basis for INMETRO certification; aligned with ISO 8124; NM 300-3 limits heavy metal migration

regulation_name: Regulamentação química — ftalatos e metais pesados (BR)
key_requirements: Phthalate restrictions aligned with international standards; heavy metal migration via NM 300-3; formaldehyde in textiles
notes: ANVISA has jurisdiction over chemical safety in products; align with INMETRO certification requirements

regulation_name: Reglamento Técnico Mercosur — Seguridad de Juguetes
regulation_ref: GMC/RES 53/96 + GMC/RES 08/14 — Reglamento Técnico Mercosur (RTM) de Seguridad de Juguetes
enforcement_body: SRT (Secretaría de Relaciones con Tutores) + IRAM
key_requirements: IRAM certification or equivalence; age grading; small parts test; chemical migration; labeling in Spanish; MERCOSUR S mark
notes: Argentina follows MERCOSUR technical regulation; IRAM testing required; MERCOSUR S mark (Sello de Seguridad) for toys

regulation_name: Resolución 203/2021 — Seguridad de productos para niños (IRAM)
key_requirements: Safety certification for toys and children's products; small parts; age grading; chemical requirements; labeling in Spanish; IRAM safety mark
notes: Argentina requires IRAM certification for toys; chemical restrictions aligned with ISO 8124-3

regulation_name: Reglamento Técnico de Seguridad de Juguetes (Chile)
regulation_ref: Decreto Supremo N° 114/2005 MINSAL + Decreto N° 47 (updated)
key_requirements: Age grading; small parts test; flammability; chemical migration; labeling in Spanish; ISP registration for toys with chemicals of concern
notes: Chile follows MERCOSUR/ISO standards; ISP oversees chemical safety; labeling must be in Spanish with age grading

regulation_name: Norma NCh-ISO 8124 — Chile toy safety adoption
key_requirements: Technical requirements aligned with ISO 8124; small parts; flammability; heavy metal migration; age category labeling
notes: Chile adopts ISO 8124 as national standard; basis for conformity assessment

enforcement_body: MINCIT + SIC (Superintendencia de Industria y Comercio) + INVIMA
key_requirements: Conformity assessment; age labeling; small parts test; chemical migration limits; labeling in Spanish; INVIMA registration where chemical components present
notes: Colombia requires technical regulation compliance; SIC market surveillance; ISO 8124 series referenced

key_requirements: Mechanical and physical safety; flammability; chemical migration; aligned with ISO 8124
notes: ICONTEC adopts ISO 8124 as Colombian technical standard

regulation_name: RTM Seguridad de Juguetes — Reglamento Técnico MERCOSUR
key_requirements: MERCOSUR S mark; age grading harmonised across bloc; small parts test; chemical migration limits; flammability; labeling harmonisation; mutual recognition among member states
notes: MERCOSUR RTM applies to AR BR PY UY; harmonised certification via accredited OCP; MERCOSUR S mark recognised across bloc

regulation_ref: MERCOSUR chemical harmonisation via GMC RES
key_requirements: Phthalate restrictions; heavy metal migration (ISO 8124-3 aligned); formaldehyde in textiles; age-specific chemical limits for <3 years
notes: MERCOSUR chemical harmonisation ongoing; Brazil (INMETRO/ABNT) often sets the benchmark

enforcement_body: SAMR (State Administration for Market Regulation) + CNCA
key_requirements: Mandatory: scope of application; general safety provisions; age grading; small parts test; labeling requirements in Chinese; 3C certification (China Compulsory Certification) for specific toy categories
notes: GB 6675 replaces old GB 6675-2003; toys for children <3 years have specific requirements; Chinese labeling mandatory

key_requirements: Small parts criterion (equivalent to ISO 8124-1); drop test; tensile test for attached components; bite test; squeeze test; accessible sharp edges and points
notes: GB 6675.2 is technically aligned with EN 71-1 and ISO 8124-1; critical for plush toy physical safety testing

key_requirements: Burn rate limits for toy materials including plush fabrics; ignition requirements; specific test methods for pile fabrics (similar to EN 71-2)
notes: Fabric flammability testing required; plush pile fabrics have specific test requirements

key_requirements: Heavy metal migration limits (aligned broadly with EN 71-3): Sb As Ba Cd Cr Co Cu Pb Mn Hg Ni Se Sr Sn Zn; material category approach; limits stricter for toys intended for children <36 months
notes: Chemical migration testing mandatory; accredited Chinese test lab (CNAS accredited) required for CCC

regulation_name: CCC — China Compulsory Certification (3C) for Toys
enforcement_body: CNCA (China National Certification and Accreditation Administration)
key_requirements: CCC mark mandatory for toys covered by catalog (soft toys/plush included if battery operated; for non-electronic plush CCC not required as of 2024 but GB 6675 compliance mandatory); CCC application via CNCA-designated certification body
notes: Note: pure non-electronic plush toys may not require CCC mark BUT must comply with GB 6675 series mandatorily; verify CCC catalog applicability for each product configuration

regulation_name: GB/T 35612 — Organic chemical compounds in toys (China)
key_requirements: Limits on organic chemical compounds: formaldehyde; azo dyes; phthalates; polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAH); preservatives; aligned broadly with EN 71-9
notes: Voluntary standard but increasingly referenced in procurement; phthalate limits and formaldehyde limits for fabric and stuffing

regulation_name: Toys and Children's Products Safety Ordinance
regulation_ref: Cap. 424 — Toys and Children's Products Safety Ordinance (as amended)
enforcement_body: Hong Kong Customs and Excise Department + EMSD
key_requirements: General Product Safety requirements; EN 71 or equivalent international standards accepted; CE marking generally accepted as evidence of compliance; age grading; small parts warning; labeling in Chinese and English
notes: Hong Kong accepts international standards (EN 71 ISO 8124); no specific HK toy standard; CE marking provides presumption of compliance; Customs&Excise enforces

regulation_name: Consumer Goods Safety Ordinance (CGSO) — Chemical Safety
key_requirements: General safety requirements including chemical safety; lead restrictions; phthalate restrictions aligned with international standards; product recall authority
notes: CGSO provides general framework; no specific HK toy chemical standard; international compliance (EN 71 or equivalent) accepted

enforcement_body: BSMI (Bureau of Standards Metrology and Inspection)
key_requirements: 商品檢驗 (commodity inspection): BSMI registration; age grading; small parts test; CNS compliance; labeling in Traditional Chinese; age warning ≤3 years
notes: Taiwan requires BSMI commodity inspection; toys must pass CNS 4797 testing; Traditional Chinese labeling mandatory

key_requirements: Flammability test equivalent to ISO 8124-2; heavy metal migration equivalent to ISO 8124-3; phthalate restrictions aligned with international standards
notes: BSMI commodity inspection covers all CNS standards; testing by BSMI-accredited lab required

key_requirements: BSMI registration mark (商標) on product/packaging; Traditional Chinese instruction manual; importer/manufacturer identified; origin labeling
notes: BSMI registration is mandatory for toys in Taiwan; R-mark or batch-by-batch inspection depending on product classification

regulation_name: Food Sanitation Law — Toys for Children Under 6
regulation_ref: Food Sanitation Law (食品衛生法) Article 62 + Ministerial Notification 336
enforcement_body: Ministry of Health Labour and Welfare (MHLW)
key_requirements: Toys for children under 6 years old regulated under Food Sanitation Law (not a toy-specific law); chemical restrictions: formaldehyde ≤75 μg/g in rubber/plastic; heavy metal migration limits; phthalate limits in vinyl; chromium nickel limits
notes: Japan's unique approach: toys for <6 use Food Sanitation Law; specific limits on formaldehyde especially relevant for plush fabric/stuffing; no general toy safety law like EU

regulation_name: ST Mark — Safety Toy (Japan) — voluntary standard
regulation_ref: ST 2015 + ST Basic Standard (日本玩具協会)
enforcement_body: Japan Toy Association (日本玩具協会)
key_requirements: Voluntary safety mark; ST-01 (mechanical safety); ST-02 (flammability); ST-04 (chemical analysis); age grading; small parts test; commercially essential for major Japanese retailers
notes: ST mark is voluntary but commercially essential for retail in Japan; covers mechanical safety flammability and chemical safety; age grading with small parts warning

regulation_name: Act on Household Goods Quality Labeling — Toy Labeling
regulation_ref: 家庭用品品質表示法 (Act No. 104/1962 as amended)
key_requirements: Quality labeling requirements for toys: material composition; country of origin; manufacturer/importer; care instructions; age grading; Japanese language mandatory
notes: Japanese Consumer Affairs Agency enforces quality labeling; all consumer product labels must be in Japanese

regulation_name: 어린이제품 공통안전기준 — Children's Products Common Safety Standard
regulation_ref: 고시 제2021-25호 — 어린이제품 공통안전기준 (Children's Products Act §20)
enforcement_body: KCA (Korea Consumer Agency) + KATS (Korean Agency for Technology and Standards)
key_requirements: KC mark mandatory; KPS (Korean Product Safety) certification; age grading; small parts test; chemical migration; heavy metal limits; phthalate restrictions; labeling in Korean
notes: KC mark is the mandatory conformity mark for toys in Korea; covers both safety and chemical requirements; third-party KC-certification body required

regulation_name: Quality Management and Safety Control of Industrial Products Act — Toy KC
regulation_ref: 산업제품 품질 관리 및 안전 관리에 관한 법률 (Enforcement Decree §26)
key_requirements: KC mark application; conformity assessment by KC-accredited body; market surveillance; product registration; labeling requirements (Korean language)
notes: Korea requires certification by KCA-designated conformity assessment body; KC mark cannot be self-declared for children's toys

regulation_name: 어린이제품 특별법 화학물질 기준 — Chemical requirements KC
regulation_ref: Children's Products Safety Act + KC Chemical Standard (KATS Notice 2021)
key_requirements: Phthalate limits ≤0.1% for DEHP DBP BBP DIBP DINP; heavy metal migration; formaldehyde in textiles ≤75 ppm; nickel release limits; azo dye ban; aligned broadly with EN 71 / ISO 8124
notes: Korean chemical limits for children's products among strictest in Asia; formaldehyde limit in textiles applies to plush fabric

enforcement_body: CPIB (Consumer Products Innovation Bureau) + Enterprise Singapore
key_requirements: SAFETY MARK mandatory for toys (schedule product); conformity assessment by PSB-approved third-party lab; EN 71 or ISO 8124 or ASTM F963 accepted as equivalent standards; age grading; small parts warning; English labeling
notes: Singapore Safety Mark (s-MARK) mandatory for toys; based on accepted international standards; PSB-accredited lab testing required

regulation_name: Singapore Chemical Safety — Hazardous Substances Act toys
key_requirements: Chemical restrictions; heavy metal migration; phthalate limits aligned with international standards; AH&S Act provisions for lead and cadmium
notes: Singapore's chemical restrictions align with international standards; no unique national toy chemical standard beyond CPSR

regulation_ref: MS ISO 8124-1; MS ISO 8124-2; MS ISO 8124-3 (SIRIM adoption)
enforcement_body: SIRIM (Standards and Industrial Research Institute of Malaysia) + KPDNHEP
key_requirements: SIRIM certification mark (MS mark) for toys; age grading; small parts test; chemical migration; flammability; labeling requirements in Bahasa Malaysia and English
notes: Malaysia adopts ISO 8124 as national standard MS ISO 8124; SIRIM accreditation required; KPDNHEP enforces consumer product safety

regulation_ref: Consumer Protection Act 1999 (CPA) + Consumer Protection (Product Safety Requirements) Regulations 2007
key_requirements: Product safety requirements; age labeling; material safety; manufacturer/importer identification; Bahasa Malaysia labeling for warnings
notes: CPA provides general product safety framework; toy-specific requirements via SIRIM MS standards

key_requirements: Compulsory standard: TISI mark (มอก.685) mandatory for toys; mechanical and physical safety; chemical migration; flammability; small parts test; age grading; labeling in Thai; TISI-accredited lab testing
notes: TIS 685 is mandatory for toys in Thailand including plush toys for children; TISI mark must appear on product/packaging; Thai language labeling required

regulation_name: Hazardous Substance Act — Chemical restrictions toys (TH)
key_requirements: Lead and cadmium restrictions; phthalate limits under Notification 2556 (2013); chemical migration aligned with ISO 8124-3
notes: Thailand chemical restrictions for toys implemented via HSA notifications; limits generally aligned with ISO 8124

regulation_name: QCVN 3:2019/BKHCN — Technical Regulation for Toy Safety
enforcement_body: Ministry of Science and Technology (MOST) + STAMEQ
key_requirements: Mandatory conformity assessment; STAMEQ certification; age grading; small parts test; chemical migration; flammability; labeling in Vietnamese; Vietnam Quality Mark
notes: QCVN 3 is mandatory national technical regulation for toys in Vietnam; ISO 8124 series referenced; certification via STAMEQ-accredited body

regulation_name: Hóa chất trong đồ chơi trẻ em — Chemical Safety (VN)
key_requirements: Heavy metal migration limits; phthalate restrictions; azo dye restrictions; formaldehyde in textiles; aligned broadly with ISO 8124-3
notes: Vietnamese chemical requirements for toys aligned with ISO 8124; enforcement improving with STAMEQ capacity building

regulation_name: SNI ISO 8124 — Indonesian Toy Safety Standard
enforcement_body: BSN (Badan Standardisasi Nasional) + BPOM + Kementerian Perdagangan
key_requirements: Mandatory SNI certification; SNI mark on product; ISPO (Indonesian standard) conformity; age grading; small parts test; chemical migration; flammability; labeling in Bahasa Indonesia
notes: Indonesia requires mandatory SNI certification for toys; SNI mark must appear on packaging; BSN-accredited lab testing; Bahasa Indonesia labeling required

regulation_name: Regulasi Kimia Mainan — Peraturan Menteri Perdagangan
key_requirements: Chemical migration limits for heavy metals; phthalate restrictions; labeling requirements; product registration
notes: Indonesia tightening chemical regulations for children's products; SNI ISO 8124-3 chemical migration limits mandatory

regulation_name: PNS ISO 8124 — Philippine Toy Safety Standard + DTI MC 23-21
key_requirements: PS mark mandatory (Philippine Standard mark); BPS product certification; age grading; small parts test; chemical migration; flammability; labeling in Filipino/English; age warning ≤36 months
notes: Philippines requires PS Mark certification for toys; DTI-BPS accredited lab testing; PNS ISO 8124 aligned with ISO 8124

regulation_name: Republic Act 10620 — Children's Product Safety Act (PH)
key_requirements: Chemical safety requirements for children's products; lead limits; phthalate restrictions; market surveillance authority; product recall provisions
notes: RA 10620 provides legal basis for children's product chemical safety in Philippines; aligned with ASEAN harmonisation

regulation_ref: Toys (Quality Control) Order 2020 (Ministry of Commerce and Industry)
key_requirements: Mandatory BIS certification for all toys; IS 9873 series (EN 71/ISO 8124 aligned); BIS licence and CML (Certificate of Conformity) required; age grading; small parts test; chemical migration; ISI mark on product
notes: India introduced mandatory BIS certification in 2020; significant change from previously voluntary standards; all toys (domestic and imported) must have BIS ISI mark; major compliance burden for importers

regulation_name: IS 9873 series — BIS Toy Safety Standard
key_requirements: Mechanical and physical safety (IS 9873-1 = ISO 8124-1); flammability (IS 9873-2 = ISO 8124-2); chemical migration (IS 9873-3 = ISO 8124-3); small parts test; age grading; labeling in English and Hindi; heavy metal migration limits
notes: IS 9873 series aligns with ISO 8124; BIS-accredited lab testing required; third-party certification mandatory; labeling in English and Hindi

regulation_name: BIS Chemical Restrictions — Phthalates and Heavy Metals
regulation_ref: IS 9873-3 + Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) Amendment 2022
key_requirements: Phthalate restrictions aligned with CPSIA/EU; heavy metal migration limits (IS 9873-3); formaldehyde limits in fabric; azo dye restrictions
notes: India's chemical requirements now among most comprehensive in Asia; BIS certification requires chemical testing

regulation_name: Consumer Goods (Toys for Children up to and including 36 months of age) Safety Standard
regulation_ref: Consumer Goods (Toys for Children up to and including 36 months of age) Safety Standard 2017 (Cth)
key_requirements: Mandatory safety standard specifically for toys for 0-36 months; AS/NZS ISO 8124-1 compliance; small parts prohibition; flammability; mechanical strength requirements; age grading; labeling requirements
notes: Australia has specific mandatory standard for toys for 0-36 months (not just general toy standard); ACCC actively enforces; mandatory recall database

regulation_name: AS/NZS ISO 8124 — Australian/New Zealand Toy Safety Standard
key_requirements: Mechanical and physical safety; flammability; chemical migration; small parts test; adopted ISO 8124 framework
notes: AS/NZS ISO 8124 is the harmonised standard for Australia and New Zealand; used as reference for mandatory standard compliance

regulation_name: Australian Consumer Law — Unsafe goods and recalls
regulation_ref: Competition and Consumer Act 2010 Schedule 2 (Australian Consumer Law)
key_requirements: Product safety provisions; compulsory recall authority; mandatory reporting of product-related injuries; supplier liability; general safety provision (no unsafe goods)
notes: ACCC maintains active recall register; mandatory reporting of death/serious injury; Australia one of highest per-capita recall rates for toys

key_requirements: AS/NZS ISO 8124 compliance mandatory; age grading; small parts test; flammability; chemical migration; labeling requirements; CE or ASTM F963 compliance as alternative pathway
notes: NZ toy regulations reference AS/NZS ISO 8124; mandatory compliance; MBIE enforces Consumer Guarantees Act and Fair Trading Act

regulation_name: Fair Trading Act + Consumer Guarantees Act — Toy Safety (NZ)
regulation_ref: Fair Trading Act 1986 + Consumer Guarantees Act 1993
key_requirements: General product safety obligations; fit for purpose; manufacturer liability; age labeling; material disclosure; mandatory safety information for toys for <3 years
notes: NZ relies on AS/NZS standards + general consumer law; labeling age grading warning mandatory

regulation_name: ASEAN Toy Safety Standard — ACCSQ Harmonisation
regulation_ref: ACCSQ Product Working Group — ASEAN Harmonised Toy Safety Standard (based on ISO 8124)
enforcement_body: ASEAN Committee on Consumer Protection (ACCP) + ACCSQ
key_requirements: Harmonisation framework for toy safety across ASEAN members; ISO 8124-1/2/3 as reference; mutual recognition of test reports; common age grading and labeling requirements
notes: ASEAN harmonisation ongoing; individual member states still maintain own mandatory requirements; regional framework provides direction for convergence

regulation_ref: ASEAN Sectoral MRA on Electrical & Electronic Equipment + chemicals harmonisation roadmap
key_requirements: Harmonisation of phthalate restrictions; heavy metal migration limits; azo dye restrictions; aligned with ISO 8124-3
notes: ASEAN chemical harmonisation for toys still largely aspirational; defer to individual country requirements

regulation_name: GCC Technical Regulation GSO 1819-1 — Toy Safety
regulation_ref: GSO 1819-1:2019 (Mechanical and Physical Properties) + GSO Technical Regulation on Toy Safety
key_requirements: IECEE CB Scheme or GCC certification; GSO 1819 series (aligned ISO 8124); age grading; small parts test; chemical migration; flammability; Arabic labeling mandatory; SASO COC required for Saudi imports
notes: GCC Technical Regulation on toys mandatory across 6 Gulf states; Arabic labeling required; SASO product certification (SALEEM program) for Saudi Arabia

regulation_name: GCC BD 131 — Chemical Requirements for Toys
regulation_ref: GSO Technical Regulation BD 131 + GSO 1819-3 (chemical migration)
key_requirements: Heavy metal migration limits aligned with ISO 8124-3; phthalate restrictions aligned with international standards; azo dye restrictions; formaldehyde limits
notes: Chemical requirements for toys in GCC; phthalate and heavy metal testing required for market access; SASO COC certificate covers chemical compliance

regulation_name: SASO CoC + SALEEM Program — Saudi Arabia Toy Import
regulation_ref: SASO Circular 2018 + SALEEM electronic approval system
enforcement_body: SASO (Saudi Standards Metrology and Quality Organization)
key_requirements: Certificate of Conformity (CoC) mandatory for toys imported into Saudi Arabia; SASO-approved testing lab; SALEEM online approval; Arabic labeling; age grading; safety warnings in Arabic
notes: Saudi Arabia (largest GCC market) requires SASO CoC via SALEEM system; additional requirements beyond GSO standards; Arabic language mandatory

regulation_ref: SI 1234 (Standards Institution of Israel — toy safety; aligned with EN 71)
enforcement_body: SII (Standards Institution of Israel) + ILNAS + Ministry of Economy
key_requirements: Mandatory standard; CE marking accepted for EU-compliant toys (bilateral recognition); SII conformity mark; age grading; small parts test; Hebrew labeling mandatory; chemical migration
notes: Israel accepts CE marking for toys as equivalent; SII mandatory standard SI 1234 aligned with EN 71; Hebrew labeling required

regulation_name: Israeli Consumer Products Safety Law — Chemical Restrictions
regulation_ref: Consumer Products Safety Law 5762-2001 (Israel) + SII chemical standards
key_requirements: Phthalate restrictions; heavy metal migration; lead paint ban; aligned with EN 71-3 and REACH
notes: Israel largely follows EU/EN 71 framework; REACH-aligned chemical restrictions increasingly adopted

regulation_name: ES 1322 — Egyptian Standard for Toy Safety
key_requirements: Mandatory conformity; GOEIC conformity certificate required for imports; age grading; small parts test; chemical migration; Arabic and English labeling; GOEIC inspection at port
notes: Egypt requires GOEIC certificate of conformity for imported toys; ES 1322 aligned with ISO 8124; port inspection common

regulation_ref: Law 67/2006 Consumer Protection Law + Ministerial Decree on toy safety
key_requirements: General product safety; labeling requirements in Arabic; manufacturer/importer identification; age grading; hazard warnings
notes: Egypt's consumer protection law requires Arabic labeling; CPA has enforcement authority for toy safety

regulation_ref: NM 03.4.001 (aligned with EN 71 / ISO 8124) + NM ISO 8124 adoption
key_requirements: Mandatory conformity marking (NM mark); IMANOR or accredited lab testing; age grading; small parts test; chemical migration; labeling in Arabic and French; CE marking often accepted as evidence of compliance
notes: Morocco aligns toy standards with EU (EN 71) and ISO 8124; CE marking largely accepted; French and Arabic labeling required

regulation_name: Loi 24-09 — Sécurité des produits et des services (Maroc)
regulation_ref: Loi n° 24-09 relative à la sécurité des produits et des services (2011)
key_requirements: Obligations générales de sécurité; marquage d'âge; avertissements en arabe et français; responsabilité fabricant/importateur; rappels
notes: Loi 24-09 provides general product safety framework; toy regulations derived from NM standards; French/Arabic bilingual requirement

regulation_ref: VC 8052 (issued under the National Regulator for Compulsory Specifications Act)
key_requirements: NRCS Letter of Authority (LOA) mandatory for toys; SABS ISO 8124 compliance; age grading; small parts test; chemical migration; flammability; English labeling; importer LOA registration
notes: South Africa requires NRCS LOA for all toys imported and sold; compliance with SABS (ISO 8124) mandatory; LOA obtained prior to importation

regulation_name: SABS ISO 8124 — South African Toy Safety Standard
regulation_ref: SABS ISO 8124-1; SABS ISO 8124-2; SABS ISO 8124-3
enforcement_body: SABS (South African Bureau of Standards) + NRCS
key_requirements: Mechanical and physical safety; flammability; chemical migration; small parts test; age grading; English labeling; NRCS LoA
notes: SABS adopts ISO 8124 as national standard; testing at SABS or accredited lab; basis for NRCS VC 8052 compulsory specification

regulation_ref: SON (Standards Organisation of Nigeria) NIS ISO 8124 adoption
key_requirements: SON Product Certification Scheme (MANCAP mark); NIS ISO 8124 standards; age grading; small parts test; chemical migration; English labeling
notes: Nigeria requires MANCAP certification for consumer goods including toys; SON enforcement at ports; NIS ISO 8124 aligned with international standards

regulation_name: Consumer Protection Framework — Nigeria Toy Safety
regulation_ref: FCCPA (Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Act) 2019 + NAFDAC
key_requirements: General product safety; chemical restrictions (NAFDAC); labeling requirements; age grading; consumer complaint mechanism
notes: Nigerian enforcement improving under FCCPC; NAFDAC involvement for chemical aspects; SON MANCAP primary certification route

regulation_name: KS ISO 8124 — Kenya Standard for Toy Safety
regulation_ref: KS ISO 8124-1; KS ISO 8124-2; KS ISO 8124-3 (KEBS adoption)
key_requirements: Diamond Mark of Quality (KEBS certification); age grading; small parts test; chemical migration; flammability; English labeling; KEBS import inspection
notes: Kenya requires KEBS Diamond Mark for toys; KS ISO 8124 aligned with ISO 8124; KEBS conducts import inspection; Pre-Export Verification of Conformity (PVoC) scheme

regulation_name: Kenya Pre-Export Verification of Conformity (PVoC) — Toys
regulation_ref: KEBS PVoC Program (applicable to toy imports)
key_requirements: PVoC certificate of conformity required before shipment to Kenya; third-party inspection in country of origin; testing to KS ISO 8124; consignment cannot clear customs without PVoC CoC
notes: Kenya PVoC scheme is operationally critical — requires pre-shipment inspection and CoC; applies to toys; third-party inspectors (SGS Bureau Veritas Intertek) conduct PVoC

key_requirements: International reference standard: small parts cylinder test; tensile and torque tests; sharp points and edges; bite and squeeze tests; accessibility tests; drop test; age grading criteria; basis for all national toy standards
notes: ISO 8124-1 is the global technical baseline; used by AU NZ IN MY VN ID PH as direct national adoption; also basis for EN 71-1 GB 6675-2 ASTM F963

key_requirements: Flammability requirements for toy materials; burn rate test; ignition resistance; specific pile fabric test; applicable to plush toys; surface burning rate limits
notes: ISO 8124-2 is the international flammability reference; used as basis for EN 71-2 GB 6675.3 and national standards; plush pile fabric specific test

key_requirements: Heavy metal migration limits (Sb As Ba Cd Cr(VI) Co Cu Pb Mn Hg Ni Se Sr Sn Zn) by material category; dry brittle powder liquid categories; basis for EN 71-3 GB 6675.4 IS 9873-3 etc.; strictest limits for scraped materials in toys for <36 months
notes: ISO 8124-3 is the global chemical migration reference; cadmium and lead limits extremely restrictive; all major markets use this or derived standard for heavy metal testing

key_requirements: Specific to playground/outdoor equipment; not directly applicable to plush but part of ISO 8124 framework
notes: ISO 8124-4 not applicable to plush toys; included for completeness of ISO 8124 framework reference

regulation_name: ISO 8124-6:2023 — Graphical Symbols for Age Warning Labels
key_requirements: Age warning symbols; internationally harmonised graphical symbols for: small parts warning (cylinder symbol); age grading; safety warnings; used in EU (EN 71 Annex V) US (ASTM F963) and globally
notes: Critical for plush 0-3 ans: the small parts warning cylinder symbol must appear on packaging per most jurisdictions; ISO 8124-6 provides the harmonised symbol specifications

regulation_name: Codex Alimentarius — N/A for toy safety
key_requirements: Codex Alimentarius covers food safety standards; not applicable to non-food toys; occasionally referenced for food-contact toy accessories (teethers mouthed items) but plush toys do not fall under Codex scope
notes: Codex not applicable to plush toys; included for completeness of 50-jurisdiction coverage

regulation_name: UNECE WP.29 — N/A for toy safety
enforcement_body: UNECE World Forum for Harmonization of Vehicle Regulations
key_requirements: UNECE WP.29 covers vehicle safety regulations; not applicable to toys; no UNECE framework for toy product safety
notes: UNECE not applicable to plush toys; included for completeness of 50-jurisdiction coverage

key_requirements: WADA governs anti-doping in sport; not applicable to toys or children's products
notes: WADA not applicable to plush toys; included for completeness of 50-jurisdiction coverage

regulation_name: OECD Product Safety Guidelines — soft reference
regulation_ref: OECD Guidelines for Multinational Enterprises + OECD Consumer Product Safety Policy Guidance
key_requirements: OECD provides policy guidance on consumer product safety (recalls cooperation; product safety in e-commerce); OECD Global Recall portal; not binding but referenced by national authorities for recall coordination; OECD guidance on toy chemicals and endocrine disruptors
notes: OECD not a binding regulator; global recall portal (globalrecalls.oecd.org) is useful for monitoring toy recalls across jurisdictions; OECD endocrine disruptor guidance may influence future toy chemical restrictions

key_requirements: Classification Annexe VIII Règle 1 → Classe I; Declaration de conformité; Technical Documentation (TD); CE marking; auto-certification sans organisme notifié; labeling per Annexe I; General Safety and Performance Requirements (GSPR)
notes: Pansement adhésif = Classe I non-stérile non-mesurant; pas d'ON requis; autocertification suffisante; TD obligatoire

key_requirements: Système UDI obligatoire (Basic UDI-DI + UDI-DI + UDI-PI); enregistrement opérateur économique dans EUDAMED; déclaration de conformité UE téléversée; surveillance post-commercialisation
notes: Phase de déploiement EUDAMED en cours; UDI obligatoire pour Classe I depuis 26 mai 2025

regulation_name: ISO 13485 — Système de management de la qualité (UE)
key_requirements: QMS couvrant conception fabrication contrôle qualité; obligatoire de facto pour TD MDR; traçabilité lots; gestion réclamations; CAPA; revue de direction
notes: Pas obligatoire formellement pour Cl. I mais exigé de facto par clients distributeurs et pour robustesse TD

key_requirements: Même exigences MDR qu'au niveau UE; Responsible Person (mandataire si hors UE); notification incidents graves à ANSM; surveillance du marché par ANSM; étiquetage en français obligatoire
notes: France = État membre UE; pas de régulation nationale additionnelle distincte pour DM Cl. I

regulation_ref: Code de la Santé Publique Art. L5212-2
key_requirements: Déclaration obligatoire incidents graves et risques graves au délai ANSM; suivi des FSCA (Field Safety Corrective Actions); portail de déclaration ANSM
notes: Spécificité FR: matériovigilance nationale en sus du système UE EUDAMED

key_requirements: CE marking Class I; MPDG as national implementation act; German-language labeling; market surveillance by Länder authorities; incident reporting via national portal
National implementing legislation for MDR; vigilance reporting; national competent authority procedures; economic operators registration
CE marking Class I; Italian-language labeling; incident reporting to Ministero della Salute; ANSM analog = ISS/Min Salute
CE marking Class I; Spanish-language labeling; AEMPS market surveillance; registro EUDAMED; vigilancia y notificacion incidentes graves
CE marking Class I; Dutch-language labeling; CIBG registration for economic operators; IGJ market surveillance; incident reporting

regulation_ref: UK Medical Devices Regulations 2002 SI 2002/618 (as amended 2019/791)
key_requirements: UKCA mark for Great Britain (CE still accepted transitionally to June 2028); Class I self-declaration; UK Responsible Person required; labeling must include UK RP address
notes: Post-Brexit: UKCA replaces CE for GB market; Northern Ireland → CE/UKNI dual marking under Windsor Framework

key_requirements: Registration on MHRA registration portal; UK Responsible Person must register; annual renewal; device listing including UDI information
QMS certification recommended for UK market; MHRA expects quality management aligned with ISO 13485; required by most UK distributors
Adoption du MDR UE par équivalence via accord MRA révisé; enregistrement auprès de Swissmedic; CH Représentant autorisé requis si fabricant hors CH; étiquetage CH-RE; système UDI aligné MDR
notes: Swissmedic reconnaît la certification CE/MDR; accord MRA CH-UE non totalement actualisé → vigilance requise

key_requirements: Enregistrement fabricants et importateurs dans base Swissmedic; Mandataire suisse (CH-REP) obligatoire pour fabricants non-CH; notification incidents graves à Swissmedic
Même exigences MDR via accord EEE; CE marking valide; Class I auto-certification; Norwegian-language labeling recommended; vigilance reporting to NOMA
notes: Norvège = EEE/EEA: MDR directement applicable via accord EEE; NOMA = autorité compétente

regulation_name: EAEU Decision 46 — Règlement commun dispositifs médicaux
key_requirements: Enregistrement DM dans le Registre EAEU unifié; classification Classe I → procédure simplifiée; dossier technique + études de sécurité; marquage EAEU; étiquetage en russe
notes: EAEU = Union économique eurasiatique (RU BY KZ AM KG); enregistrement EAEU valide dans tous les États membres

key_requirements: Enregistrement certificat d'État russe; dossier en russe; tests locaux possibles; renouvellement périodique; vigilance nationale
notes: Contexte géopolitique 2024: vérifier sanctions et restrictions export applicables

key_requirements: Enregistrement unifié EAEU valable RU/BY/KZ/AM/KG; classification Cl.I; dossier technique commun; étiquetage multilingue (russe + langues nationales); système UDI EAEU en développement

enforcement_body: TITCK (Turkish Medicines and Medical Devices Agency)
key_requirements: Enregistrement dans ÜTS (Ürün Takip Sistemi); déclaration conformité Classe I; mandataire turc requis; étiquetage en turc; vigilance TITCK; UDI via ÜTS
notes: Turquie aligne sa réglementation sur MDR 2017/745; ÜTS est le registre national obligatoire pour tous les DM

regulation_name: Ukraine — Enregistrement DM auprès du MoH
regulation_ref: Order of MoH Ukraine No. 753/2016 + post-2022 updates
enforcement_body: Ministry of Health Ukraine / State Expert Center
key_requirements: Enregistrement national auprès du MoH Ukraine; certificat d'homologation; dossier en ukrainien; Classe I → procédure simplifiée
notes: Contexte guerre: réglementations allégées temporaires possibles; UE aide à harmonisation future

regulation_name: FDA 21 CFR Part 880.5240 — Adhesive bandage (Class I 510(k) Exempt)
key_requirements: Adhesive bandage classified as Class I device; 510(k) Exempt (General Controls only); no premarket submission required; General Controls: establishment registration; device listing; labeling; GMP/QSR; prohibited acts
notes: Exemption from 510(k) premarket notification; General Controls apply

regulation_name: FDA Establishment Registration + Device Listing (21 CFR 807)
key_requirements: Annual establishment registration with FDA (Oct 1-Dec 31); device listing (Form FDA 3881); US Agent required for foreign establishments; registration fee applicable

regulation_name: FDA UDI Final Rule — 21 CFR Part 801 Subpart B
key_requirements: UDI label requirement: Device Identifier (DI) + Production Identifier (PI); GUDID database submission; barcode or RFID on label; Class I labeling compliance

regulation_name: FDA QSR 21 CFR Part 820 — Quality System Regulation
regulation_ref: 21 CFR Part 820 (harmonizing with ISO 13485 via QMSR 2024)
key_requirements: Design controls; document controls; CAPA; complaint handling; production controls; labeling controls; distribution records; QSR now aligned with ISO 13485:2016 per FDA 2024 rule effective Feb 2026

regulation_name: Proposition 65 — Warning si latex / produits chimiques
regulation_ref: California Safe Drinking Water and Toxic Enforcement Act of 1986 (Prop 65)
key_requirements: Avertissement obligatoire si produit contient substances listées Prop 65 (latex naturel; adhésifs contenant DEHP ou autres); avertissement sur emballage ou point de vente; seuils de minimis applicables
notes: Applicable si pansement contient latex naturel ou substances chimiques listées Prop 65; vérifier composition adhésif

regulation_name: NY — Federal FDA regulations + State Health Law
key_requirements: Federal FDA regulations apply; NY State has no additional Class I medical device specific requirements; standard product liability and labeling laws apply
notes: NY state follows federal FDA framework for Class I medical devices

regulation_name: Health Canada — Medical Device Class I (MDEL required for importers)
regulation_ref: Food and Drugs Act + Medical Devices Regulations SOR/98-282
key_requirements: Class I devices exempt from Medical Device Licence (MDL); MDEL (Medical Device Establishment Licence) required for importers and distributors; ISO 13485 QMS required for MDEL holders; bilingual labeling F/A required
notes: Fabricant: MDL non requis pour Cl. I; distributeur/importateur: MDEL obligatoire; ISO 13485 exigé pour MDEL

key_requirements: Bilingual labeling (French/English) required; lot number; expiry if applicable; name and address of Canadian importer/distributor; ISO 13485 QMS certificate required for establishment licence

regulation_name: COFEPRIS — Registro Sanitario Clase I (Dispositivo Médico)
regulation_ref: NOM-137-SSA1-2008 + Reglamento de Insumos para la Salud
enforcement_body: COFEPRIS (Comisión Federal para la Protección contra Riesgos Sanitarios)
key_requirements: Registro Sanitario obligatoire auprès COFEPRIS; dossier technique en espagnol; classification Clase I; étiquetage en espagnol; BPF (Buenas Prácticas de Fabricación); renouvellement 5 ans
Étiquetage en espagnol; nom du dispositif; fabricant; pays d'origine; numéro de lot; date de péremption; mode d'emploi; contre-indications; conditions de conservation

regulation_name: ANVISA RDC 751/2022 — Enregistrement DM Classe I
key_requirements: Notificação (Classe I) auprès ANVISA; dossier simplifié; taxe ANVISA; renouvellement quinquennal; étiquetage en portugais; représentant légal brésilien requis
notes: RDC 751/2022 remplace RDC 185/2001; Notificação pour Cl. I = procédure allégée

regulation_name: ANVISA RDC 16/2013 — BPF (Bonnes Pratiques de Fabrication)
key_requirements: Bonnes pratiques de fabrication pour DM; QMS aligné ISO 13485; obligatoire pour obtenir Certificado de Boas Práticas de Fabricação (CBPF); inspections possibles

regulation_name: ANMAT — Enregistrement Producto Médico Clase I
enforcement_body: ANMAT (Administración Nacional de Medicamentos Alimentos y Tecnología Médica)
key_requirements: Registro de Producto Médico en ANMAT; Clase I = bajo riesgo; representante legal argentino requis; dossier en espagnol; GMP certificate from country of origin

regulation_name: ISP Chile — Registro sanitario dispositivo médico
enforcement_body: ISP (Instituto de Salud Pública de Chile)
key_requirements: Registro sanitario auprès ISP; Clase I = bajo riesgo; dossier technique; représentant technique en Chile; étiquetage en espagnol; renouvellement 5 ans

enforcement_body: INVIMA (Instituto Nacional de Vigilancia de Medicamentos y Alimentos)
key_requirements: Registro Sanitario Clase I auprès INVIMA; FSOL (Fabricante en Solicitud de Libre Venta); Clase I = bajo riesgo; dossier en espagnol; fabricante o representante local

regulation_name: MERCOSUR GMC Resolution 40/00 — Cadre enregistrement DM
key_requirements: Cadre harmonisé pour enregistrement DM dans MERCOSUR (BR AR UY PY); classification par risque; procédures simplifiées Classe I; reconnaissance mutuelle enregistrements entre pays membres en cours
notes: Harmonisation partielle; chaque pays maintient son propre registre national (ANVISA ANMAT etc.)

regulation_name: NMPA — Filing DM Classe I (备案 Bèi'àn)
regulation_ref: Regulations on Supervision and Administration of Medical Devices (2021) + NMPA Order 47
key_requirements: Classe I → 备案 (filing/enregistrement simplifié); dépôt auprès NMPA local ou provincial; dossier technique; étiquetage en chinois simplifié obligatoire; agent chinois requis; surveillance post-marché
notes: Distinction: Cl. I = Filing (备案) vs Cl. II/III = Registration (注册); procédure allégée mais obligatoire

key_requirements: Si pansement contient composants textiles médicaux: GB 15979 pour produits d'hygiène jetables applicable; vérifier classification matière adhésive et base textile
notes: Applicable si formulation inclut textile médical; pansement adhésif standard Cl. I non-stérile → vérifier pertinence

key_requirements: Étiquetage obligatoire en chinois simplifié; nom générique DM; numéro NMPA filing; fabricant; importateur en Chine; date de fabrication; durée d'utilisation; conditions de conservation; notice d'utilisation

regulation_name: Hong Kong — Medical Devices Ordinance Cap. 760
regulation_ref: Medical Devices Ordinance Cap. 760 (enacted 2014 phased in 2020)
enforcement_body: Medical Device Division / Department of Health HK
key_requirements: Enregistrement DM depuis 2020 phase-in; Class A (lowest risk) = advisory listing; registration before market access; labeling in English or Chinese; local Hong Kong authorized agent

regulation_name: TFDA — Enregistrement DM Classe I Taiwan
key_requirements: Class I (lowest risk) = notification/registration auprès TFDA; Taiwan entity as license applicant; dossier technique; étiquetage en chinois traditionnel; local agent obligatoire; renouvellement 5 ans

regulation_name: PMD Act — Notification Classe I (Todoke) PMDA
regulation_ref: Pharmaceutical and Medical Device Act (PMD Act No. 145/1960 révisé 2014)
enforcement_body: PMDA + MHLW (Ministry of Health Labour and Welfare)
key_requirements: DM Classe I (一般医療機器) = Todoke (notification simple); Marketing Authorization Holder (MAH) japonais obligatoire; GMP audit Classe I → certificat QMS requis; étiquetage en japonais
notes: Classe I = 一般医療機器; notification = Todoke (届出) simplifiée vs approbation pour Cl. III/IV

regulation_ref: Ministerial Ordinance on Standards for Manufacturing Control (QMS Ordinance No.169/2004)
key_requirements: QMS Ordinance alignée ISO 13485; certificat QMS pour DM Cl. I requis; inspection possible; traçabilité; gestion réclamations; CAPA; documentation en japonais ou traduction

regulation_ref: Medical Devices Act (Act No. 17250/2020) + MFDS Notice
enforcement_body: MFDS (Ministry of Food and Drug Safety)
key_requirements: Class I = Notification (신고) vs Permit (허가) pour Cl. II-IV; dossier simplifié; Korean Regulatory Agent obligatoire; étiquetage en coréen; renouvellement périodique

regulation_ref: Medical Devices Act Art. 22 + MFDS KGMP Standards
key_requirements: KGMP certificate obligatoire basé ISO 13485; inspection usine possible; document contrôle; CAPA; traçabilité; audit sur site ou sur dossier; renouvellement 3 ans

regulation_name: HSA — Enregistrement DM Classe A Singapore
regulation_ref: Health Products Act (Cap. 122D) + HSA MD Classification Guidance
key_requirements: Class A (lowest risk) = listing registration auprès HSA; no premarket review required but post-market obligations; Safety and Performance requirements; English labeling; importer/distributor registration
Incident reporting to HSA; product recall procedure; vigilance reporting; importers/distributors must be HSA-registered; registration number on label

regulation_name: MDA — Medical Device Act 2012 Classe A Malaysia
regulation_ref: Medical Device Act 2012 (Act 737) + Medical Device Regulations 2012
key_requirements: Classe A (lowest risk) = registration obligatoire auprès MDA; holder must be Malaysian company or authorized representative; dossier technique; labeling in Bahasa Malaysia and/or English

regulation_name: Thai FDA — Notification DM Classe I Thailand
enforcement_body: Thai FDA (Food and Drug Administration Thailand)
key_requirements: Class I = Notification (แจ้ง) obligatoire; local licensed importer required; Thai-language labeling; safety data; post-market surveillance

regulation_name: DAV — Enregistrement DM Classe A Vietnam
enforcement_body: DAV (Drug Administration of Vietnam) / Ministry of Health
key_requirements: Classe A (lowest risk) = Declaration/Listing; Vietnamese authorized representative required; Vietnamese-language labeling; local importation license; technical dossier with Vietnamese translation

regulation_name: BPOM — Notifikasi Alat Kesehatan Kelas A Indonesia
enforcement_body: BPOM (National Agency of Drug and Food Control) / Kemenkes
key_requirements: Kelas I (Class A) = Notifikasi (simplified registration); local Indonesian company or authorized distributor required; Indonesian-language labeling; Halal certification consideration; BPOM registration number on label

regulation_name: FDA-PH — Certificate of Device Registration Classe A
enforcement_body: FDA Philippines — CDRR (Center for Device Regulation Radiation Health and Research)
key_requirements: Class A (low risk) = CDR (Certificate of Device Registration); local distributor/importer as license holder; dossier technique; English labeling; GMP certificate from country of origin

regulation_name: CDSCO — Medical Device Rules 2017 Classe A India
regulation_ref: Medical Devices Rules 2017 (MDR 2017) under Drugs and Cosmetics Act
key_requirements: Class A (lowest risk) = Registration Certificate from CDSCO; Indian Authorized Agent required for foreign manufacturers; English labeling; CDSCO Technical Dossier; GMP certificate

regulation_name: CDSCO Form MD-15 — Licence importateur Inde
key_requirements: Importer licence (Form MD-15) mandatory for importing medical devices; state drug controller may also be involved; renewal annual; Class A requires registration certificate before import licence

regulation_name: TGA — ARTG Listing Class I Australia
regulation_ref: Therapeutic Goods Act 1989 + Therapeutic Goods (Medical Devices) Regulations 2002
key_requirements: Class I (lowest risk) = Listing on ARTG; self-assessment; Conformity Assessment Declaration; Essential Principles compliance; sponsor must be Australian entity; English labeling; post-market vigilance

Essential Principles (safety and performance) per Schedule 1; conformity assessment based ISO 13485; clinical evidence requirement (post-market clinical follow-up); manufacturer must hold ISO 13485 or equivalent

regulation_name: Medsafe NZ — Medical Device Notification (transition Therapeutics Products Act)
regulation_ref: Medicines Act 1981 + Medical Devices Safety Act 2021
key_requirements: Class I = lower risk notification; NZ accepts CE mark and TGA as comparable regulatory systems; transition to new Therapeutic Products Act ongoing; WAND database for provisional consent
notes: NZ en transition réglementaire; Therapeutic Products Act 2023 reporté; actuellement système provisoire acceptant CE/TGA

regulation_name: ASEAN Medical Device Directive (AMDD) — Cadre harmonisé
key_requirements: Cadre harmonisé ASEAN pour DM; classification A-D par risque; Class A = lowest risk; ASEAN Common Submission Dossier Template (CSDT); chaque État membre maintient son autorité nationale
notes: AMDD = cadre directeur; SG/MY/TH/PH/ID/VN en divers stades d'implémentation

regulation_ref: Saudi Food and Drug Authority Medical Device Interim Regulation
key_requirements: SFDA registration for all medical devices including Class I; MDMA (Medical Device Marketing Authorization); Saudi authorized agent required; Arabic/English labeling; ISO 13485 required

regulation_ref: UAE Federal Law No. 4/1983 + Ministerial Decree on Medical Devices
enforcement_body: UAE Ministry of Health (MOHAP) / Dubai Health Authority / DOH Abu Dhabi
key_requirements: Registration required in UAE; multiple authorities (federal MOH + emirate-level DHA DOH); Class I = simplified registration; local authorized agent; Arabic labeling required; GSO standards
notes: UAE = marché clé GCC; enregistrement fédéral MOHAP + éventuel enregistrement emirat (Dubai DHA Abu Dhabi DOH)

regulation_name: Ministry of Health Israel — AMAR Registration
regulation_ref: Medical Devices Ordinance (Amendment) 1974 + MoH Medical Devices Guideline
enforcement_body: MoH AMAR (Administration of Medical Devices and Accessories)
key_requirements: Registration in Israeli Medical Devices Registry; Class I = simplified pathway with CE mark recognition; Israeli Authorized Representative (IAR) required; Hebrew/English labeling; post-market vigilance to AMAR
notes: Israël reconnaît CE marking comme base pour enregistrement simplifié; IAR obligatoire pour fabricants non-IL

regulation_name: EDA — Egyptian Drug Authority Medical Device Registration
enforcement_body: EDA (Egyptian Drug Authority) — Medical Devices Center
key_requirements: Registration certificate obligatoire; Class I = low risk but registration required; Egyptian local agent mandatory; dossier in Arabic and English; Arabic labeling; GMP certificate from country of origin; 5-year validity

regulation_name: DMP Maroc — Enregistrement DM (Direction du Médicament)
regulation_ref: Loi 17-04 (Code du Médicament et de la Pharmacie) + Décrets d'application
enforcement_body: DMP (Direction du Médicament et de la Pharmacie) / Ministère de la Santé
key_requirements: Enregistrement obligatoire auprès DMP; dossier en français et/ou arabe; Classe I = bas risque; représentant marocain requis; étiquetage bilingue français/arabe; conformité CE reconnue comme base; renouvellement 5 ans

regulation_name: SAHPRA — Class A Medical Device Listing
regulation_ref: Medicines and Related Substances Act 101/1965 (as amended) + SAHPRA MD Regulations
enforcement_body: SAHPRA (South African Health Products Regulatory Authority)
key_requirements: Class A (lowest risk) = Listing registration; SAHPRA registration number on label; South African responsible person required; English labeling; Essential Performance Requirements; ISO 13485 recommended

regulation_ref: NAFDAC Act Cap N1 LFN 2004 + NAFDAC MD Guidelines
enforcement_body: NAFDAC (National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control)
key_requirements: Registration certificate from NAFDAC mandatory; local Nigerian agent required; English dossier; English labeling; GMP certificate from country of origin; Class I = low risk simplified pathway; NAFDAC number on label

regulation_name: Pharmacy and Poisons Board Kenya — MD Registration
regulation_ref: Pharmacy and Poisons Act (Cap. 244) + PPB Medical Devices Guidelines
key_requirements: Registration required for all medical devices including Class I; Kenyan authorized agent or importer required; English dossier; English labeling; GMP certificate; PPB registration number; post-market surveillance

Système de management de la qualité pour dispositifs médicaux; exigé par quasi-totalité des autorités mondiales (FDA MDR TGA PMDA MFDS Health Canada etc.); couvre conception fabrication contrôle qualité traçabilité réclamations CAPA
notes: Standard universel QMS; certification ISO 13485 facilite les enregistrements dans toutes les juridictions

key_requirements: Processus de gestion des risques pour DM; identification dangers; estimation et évaluation risques; contrôle risques; surveillance post-production; requis par MDR Annexe I FDA QSR et la plupart des autorités mondiales

Medical Device Single Audit Program (MDSAP): un audit unique couvre FDA/Health Canada/TGA/ANVISA/PMDA; réduit le nombre d'audits pour fabricants exportateurs mondiaux; participation volontaire mais avantageuse pour marché multi-juridictions
notes: MDSAP participants: US FDA Health Canada TGA Australia ANVISA Brazil PMDA Japan; certificat unique reconnu par 5 juridictions majeures

regulation_name: Codex Alimentarius — N/A pour dispositifs médicaux
key_requirements: Codex Alimentarius couvre les normes de sécurité alimentaire; non applicable aux dispositifs médicaux tels que les pansements adhésifs; inclus pour couverture exhaustive des 50 juridictions
notes: Codex non applicable aux pansements adhésifs; inclus pour complétude

regulation_name: UNECE WP.29 — N/A pour dispositifs médicaux
enforcement_body: UNECE World Forum for Harmonization of Vehicle Regulations
key_requirements: UNECE WP.29 couvre les réglementations de sécurité des véhicules; non applicable aux dispositifs médicaux ou aux pansements adhésifs
notes: UNECE non applicable aux pansements adhésifs; inclus pour complétude

key_requirements: WADA régit l'antidopage dans le sport; non applicable aux dispositifs médicaux de classe I tels que les pansements adhésifs
notes: WADA non applicable aux pansements adhésifs; inclus pour complétude

regulation_ref: OECD Guidelines for Multinational Enterprises + OECD Consumer Product Safety Policy Guidance
key_requirements: OECD fournit des orientations politiques sur la sécurité des produits de consommation (rappels coopération; sécurité produits e-commerce); portail global recalls (globalrecalls.oecd.org); non contraignant mais référencé par autorités nationales pour coordination rappels
notes: OECD non contraignant; portail global de rappels utile pour surveillance multi-juridictions des DM

enforcement_body: EC DG SANTE + Member State NCAs
key_requirements: Notification to national authority 6 months before market; max 10 ml refill/2 ml cartridge; nicotine <=20 mg/ml; child-resistant/tamper-evident packaging; no prohibited additives; annual sales reporting; cross-border notification portal; health warnings 30% of surface
notes: TPD Article 20 is the core EU ENDS regime; Member States may add restrictions (see FR/DE/IT/ES/NL entries)

key_requirements: Proposed ban on characterizing flavors in e-cigarettes (excluding tobacco flavor); aligns with existing NL/DK bans; expected adoption 2025-2026; operators must track legislative progress and reformulate products
notes: Revision in progress; adoption expected 2025-2026; flavor ban likely to cover all EU member states

key_requirements: Nicotine classified CMR 1B (reproductive toxicity); propylene glycol assessed for inhalation exposure; e-liquid manufacturers must comply with REACH restrictions on constituent substances; SDS required for professional supply; concentration limits apply
notes: Nicotine REACH classification imposes strict occupational exposure and labelling obligations across EU

regulation_name: Excise Directive — Proposed Harmonised E-liquid Excise
regulation_ref: Council Directive 2020/262/EU + EC proposal for e-liquid excise harmonisation
enforcement_body: EC DG TAXUD + Member State revenue authorities
key_requirements: Current Directive 2020/262 does not mandate e-liquid excise; EC working paper proposes harmonised minimum excise duty on e-liquids; Member States may apply national excise independently in the interim; fiscal mark requirements vary by country
notes: No harmonised EU e-liquid excise yet; national excise already applies in FR/IT/DE; watch EC legislative calendar

regulation_name: Decret 2016-1117 — Interdiction vapotage lieux publics fermes
key_requirements: Interdit le vapotage dans les espaces de travail fermes et couverts a usage collectif et dans les etablissements scolaires; sanctions contraventionnelles; distincte de l'interdiction tabac
notes: Vente libre en France sous TPD Art. 20; usage restreint dans les lieux collectifs

key_requirements: Notification 6 mois avant mise sur marche; depot sur portail europeen e-Submissions; donnees ingredients et emissions; rapport annuel des ventes; interdiction additifs aromatisants type bonbon/menthol progressivement envisagee
notes: France a transpose TPD Art. 20; taxes specifiques sur e-liquides envisagees (PLF 2025)

regulation_ref: Tabakerzeugnisgesetz vom 4. April 2016 (BGBl. I S. 569) + Novelle 2021
key_requirements: Transposition TPD Art. 20; notification Bundesamt fuer Verbraucherschutz und Lebensmittelsicherheit (BVL); flavor ban on menthol e-liquids since 2021; nicotine <=20 mg/ml; max refill 10 ml; child-proof packaging; Verpackungsgesetz (packaging recycling) applies
notes: DE Laender enforce locally; flavor restrictions stricter than minimum TPD; excise duty on e-liquids introduced Jan 2024 (EUR 0.20/ml)

regulation_ref: Tabaksteuermodernisierungsgesetz 2021 — e-liquid excise from 01.07.2022 rising to EUR 0.32/ml by 2026
key_requirements: Excise duty on nicotine-containing e-liquids: EUR 0.20/ml from Jul 2022 increasing annually to EUR 0.32/ml by Jan 2026; requires excise warehouse authorisation or registered consignee status; fiscal marks (Steuerbanderole) not required for e-liquids but tax declaration mandatory
notes: One of the highest e-liquid excise rates in EU; compliance requires fiscal planning and warehouse authorisation

enforcement_body: Ministero della Salute / ADM (Agenzia Dogane e Monopoli)
key_requirements: Transposition of TPD; notification system via ADM; nicotine e-cigarettes subject to excise duty (36.8% of equivalent cigarette price as of 2024); authorization required to sell; retail license obligatory; minor sales prohibited
notes: Italy applies high e-liquid excise; ADM controls distribution; retail must have tobacco license

regulation_name: DM Salute — Divieto aromi caratterizzanti (Flavor Ban 2024)
key_requirements: Ban on characterizing flavors in e-cigarettes (fruity sweet menthol) effective 2024; only tobacco flavor permitted; aligns Italy with NL/DK; enforcement by NAS (Carabinieri health unit)
notes: Flavor ban in force; significant reformulation required; menthol products non-compliant

key_requirements: Transposition of TPD Art. 20; notification to AEMPS 6 months before market; nicotine <=20 mg/ml; volume/size limits; health warnings; prohibition on advertising in media; CCAA (regional authorities) may add restrictions
notes: ES applies TPD minimum; no national flavor ban yet; excise on e-liquids under discussion

regulation_ref: Tabaks- en rookwarenwet + Besluit uitvoering Tabaks- en rookwarenwet (flavor ban effective Oct 2024)
enforcement_body: NVWA (Netherlands Food and Consumer Product Safety Authority)
key_requirements: CRITICAL: Comprehensive flavor ban on e-cigarettes (all flavors except tobacco) effective October 2024; nicotine <=20 mg/ml; TPD Art. 20 notifications to RIVM; advertising ban; use prohibited in hospitality venues; NVWA enforcement inspections; fines up to EUR 450000
notes: One of strictest EU e-cig regimes; flavor ban enforced with heavy fines; only tobacco-flavor e-liquids allowed

regulation_ref: SI 2016/507 — Tobacco and Related Products Regulations 2016
key_requirements: MHRA notification 6 months prior; nicotine <=20 mg/ml; max 2 ml cartridge/10 ml refill; child-resistant packaging; prohibited ingredients list; health warnings; annual reporting; disposable vapes not banned under TRPR
notes: TRPR is UK post-Brexit equivalent of TPD; MHRA runs UK notification portal; separate from Disposable Vape Ban

regulation_name: Disposable Vape Ban 2025 — Tobacco and Vapes Bill
regulation_ref: Tobacco and Vapes Bill (introduced Oct 2024) + Statutory Instrument expected 2025
key_requirements: CRITICAL: Ban on sale of single-use disposable vapes effective June 2025; manufacturers must transition to refillable/rechargeable devices; retail stock clearance deadline; compliance requires product line redesign; retailers face criminal liability
notes: Disposable vape ban in force from 1 June 2025; significant supply chain impact; retail must clear stock

key_requirements: New UK excise duty on vaping products from October 2026: GBP 2.20/10 ml for nicotine-free liquids GBP 3.00/10 ml for nicotine-containing; registration required; fiscal compliance obligations
notes: VPED announced Budget 2024; in force October 2026; operators must plan for fiscal compliance

regulation_name: Loi sur les produits du tabac (LPTab)
regulation_ref: Loi federale sur les produits du tabac et les cigarettes electroniques (LPTab) — en vigueur 1er octobre 2024
enforcement_body: OFSP (Office federal de la sante publique) / Swissmedic
key_requirements: CRITICAL: New Swiss tobacco products law in force Oct 2024; nicotine e-cigarettes regulated; notification to OFSP before market; nicotine <=20 mg/ml; advertising restrictions; no sale to under-18; flavor restrictions (similar to EU); separate regime for nicotine-free vapes
notes: Switzerland not EU member; LPTab independent of TPD but aligned; major market access change from Oct 2024

regulation_ref: Lov om vern mot tobakksskader (Tobakksskadeloven) + Forordning implementing TPD via EEA
key_requirements: Norway implements TPD Art. 20 via EEA Agreement; notification to Mattilsynet; nicotine <=20 mg/ml; flavor ban on characterizing flavors (menthol ban 2022 broader ban proposed); age 18+ only; advertising prohibited
notes: EEA member; TPD applies; flavor restrictions stricter than minimum TPD; excise duty applies

regulation_ref: Federal Law No. 15-FZ (2013) + No. 303-FZ (2019 ENDS amendment)
key_requirements: CRITICAL: ENDS regulated as tobacco products; mandatory labeling; excise duty on e-liquids (rising annually); advertising ban; no sale to minors; public vaping prohibited (same locations as tobacco); product registration required; EAEU TR TS 035/2014 applies
notes: Russia major market; strict excise + public ban; requires EAEU technical compliance; sanctions context affects market access for Western brands

regulation_ref: Tutun Urunlerinin Zararlarindan Korunma Kanunu No. 4207 + TAPDK regulations
enforcement_body: TAPDK (Tobacco and Alcohol Market Regulatory Authority)
key_requirements: All characterizing flavors banned; e-cigarettes treated as tobacco products; TAPDK license required; high excise duty; advertising prohibited; public vaping banned; import requires TAPDK approval; enforcement increased since 2022
notes: Turkey de facto restricts market heavily; flavor ban + high excise = limited market viability

key_requirements: TPD-aligned regulation adopted 2021; notification before market entry; nicotine <=20 mg/ml; child-proof packaging; public vaping ban; age 18+; advertising restrictions; war context affects enforcement capacity but law remains in force
notes: Ukraine EU candidate; regulation aligned with TPD; enforcement capacity reduced due to ongoing conflict

regulation_name: EAEU TR TS 035/2014 — Tobacco Technical Regulation
regulation_ref: EAEU Technical Regulation TR TS 035/2014 on Tobacco Products
key_requirements: CRITICAL: Mandatory technical regulation covering tobacco and ENDS products; EAC conformity mark required; product registration in EAEU; nicotine content limits; labeling in Russian/national languages; applies to Russia Belarus Kazakhstan Armenia Kyrgyzstan; EAEU-wide certification mandatory
notes: Applies to all 5 EAEU member states; EAC mark mandatory; separate from EU CE marking

regulation_name: FDA PMTA — Premarket Tobacco Product Application
regulation_ref: 21 U.S.C. Section 387j — Federal Food Drug and Cosmetic Act (FDCA)
key_requirements: CRITICAL: All ENDS products require FDA Premarket Tobacco Product Application (PMTA) authorization before legal US market access; scientific evidence of appropriate public health benefit; toxicology; clinical data; manufacturing; most flavor ENDS denied PMTA; tobacco-flavor products more likely authorized
notes: PMTA is primary FDA gateway; thousands of products on enforcement-discretion; only approx 23 authorized as of 2025; major legal barrier

regulation_name: 21 CFR Part 1140 — Sales and Distribution Restrictions
regulation_ref: 21 CFR Part 1140 — Tobacco Product Sales and Distribution
key_requirements: CRITICAL: Age verification mandatory (21+ since Dec 2019 — Tobacco 21 law); ID check for anyone appearing under 27; vending machine restrictions; no free samples; no self-service displays; retailer compliance checks by FDA
notes: Tobacco 21 federal law; retailers subject to FDA compliance checks and civil money penalties

regulation_name: PACT Act — Prevent All Cigarette Trafficking
regulation_ref: 15 U.S.C. Section 376 — PACT Act (amended 2021 to include ENDS)
key_requirements: CRITICAL: E-cigarettes covered by PACT Act since March 2021; USPS prohibited from shipping vaping products; private carriers (UPS/FedEx) also banned from residential delivery of vaping products; mandatory age-verification + sales tax collection for online sales; retailer registration with ATF
notes: PACT Act effectively bans mail-order delivery of vapes; major DTC channel disruption; compliance requires carrier agreements

regulation_name: CA Prop 56 Tobacco Tax + SB 793 Flavor Ban
regulation_ref: Proposition 56 (2016) + CA Health and Safety Code Section 119405 (SB 793)
enforcement_body: California Department of Tax and Fee Administration (CDTFA) + CDPH
key_requirements: Flavor ban on all flavored tobacco products including e-cigarettes effective Dec 2022 (SB 793 upheld after veto referendum overturned); Prop 56 excise tax on e-cigarettes; age 21+; retailer license required; violations = criminal misdemeanor
notes: CA flavor ban most impactful US state ban; covers menthol too; significant enforcement by local jurisdictions

regulation_name: NY Flavor Ban — Public Health Law Section 1399-mm
regulation_ref: NY Public Health Law Section 1399-mm + Executive Order (flavor e-cigs banned 2019 codified 2020)
key_requirements: Ban on flavored e-cigarettes (all flavors including menthol) since 2019-2020; only unflavored/tobacco-flavor permitted; age 21+ (NY law); online sales restrictions; retailer license required; penalties for violations
notes: One of first US state flavor bans; strictly enforced; significant NY market restriction

regulation_name: Tobacco and Vaping Products Act (TVPA) + Vaping Promotion Regulations
regulation_ref: Tobacco and Vaping Products Act SC 2018 c. 9 + Vaping Products Labelling and Packaging Regulations (SOR/2019-353) + Vaping Products Promotion Regulations (SOR/2019-354)
key_requirements: CRITICAL: Federal licensing required for vaping product manufacturers/importers; max nicotine 20 mg/ml; child-resistant packaging; health warnings covering 75% of principal display surface; advertising restrictions; no promotion to minors; flavors restricted to tobacco/mint/menthol at federal level; provinces may add restrictions
notes: Canada major market; TVPA robust framework; Quebec and Nova Scotia have additional flavor bans; excise duty on vaping products since Oct 2022

regulation_name: Excise Act — Vaping Products Excise Duty
key_requirements: Excise duty on vaping products: CAD $1/2 ml (first 10 ml) + $1/10 ml thereafter; excise stamps required; licensed manufacturers/importers; provincial additional excise in most provinces
notes: Excise stamps mandatory; coordinated federal-provincial excise compliance required

regulation_ref: Decreto DOF 19-02-2020 + reaffirme Decreto DOF 2024 (prohibition importation/commercialisation ENDS)
key_requirements: BANNED: Mexico bans importation sale distribution and promotion of e-cigarettes and ENDS since 2020 decree upheld and confirmed in 2024; COFEPRIS enforcement; customs seizure; criminal penalties for commercial violation; personal use in grey area but officially prohibited
notes: Full commercial ban; high customs seizure risk; COFEPRIS actively enforces; black market exists but non-compliant

regulation_ref: Resolucao ANVISA RDC n 855 de 25 de julho de 2024 (update of RDC 46/2009)
key_requirements: BANNED: Commercial sale and import of e-cigarettes and ENDS banned in Brazil; ANVISA prohibition maintained in updated RDC 855/2024; personal possession tolerated but commercial activity prohibited; significant penalty for violations; major customs enforcement at ports
notes: Brazil long-standing ban confirmed 2024; no legal market; enforcement at customs; grey market exists

enforcement_body: ANMAT (Administracion Nacional de Medicamentos Alimentos y Tecnologia Medica)
key_requirements: BANNED: Commercial import sale and distribution of e-cigarettes prohibited by ANMAT since 2011; prohibition maintained and confirmed; grey market products circulate but no legal commercial pathway; enforcement at federal and provincial level
notes: Long-standing ban; no legal commercial market; ANMAT confirmed ban multiple times

regulation_ref: Resolucion Exenta N 1023 del Ministerio de Salud (2021) + Ley 20.660 (Tabaco)
key_requirements: E-cigarettes regulated under tobacco framework; nicotine products require ISP (Instituto de Salud Publica) registration; nicotine <=20 mg/ml; health warnings; advertising restrictions; public vaping prohibited same as tobacco; age 18+
notes: Chile allows with restrictions; ISP registration required; aligned with tobacco law

regulation_ref: Ley 1335 de 2009 (Prevencion del tabaquismo) + Decreto 1076 reglamentario
enforcement_body: Ministerio de Salud y Proteccion Social / INVIMA
key_requirements: Vaping products regulated as tobacco products under Ley 1335; INVIMA registration required; advertising prohibited; public vaping banned; age 18+; nicotine e-liquids may be classified as pharmaceutical products requiring INVIMA pharmaceutical registration
notes: Colombia regulatory path uncertain; INVIMA classification as pharma or tobacco product varies; consult local counsel

regulation_name: Mercosur — pas de cadre harmonise ENDS
regulation_ref: N/A — Mercosur does not have harmonized ENDS/vaping regulation
key_requirements: No Mercosur-level harmonized regulation for e-cigarettes; member states apply national rules independently; BR and AR ban commercial sale; PY and UY have partial frameworks; bloc-level harmonization not expected in near term
notes: Mercosur bloc has no ENDS harmonisation; national rules dominate; included for jurisdictional completeness

regulation_name: STMA Order No. 1 (2022) — E-cigarettes regulated as tobacco
regulation_ref: STMA Regulations on E-cigarettes (Guo Yan Fa No. 1 of 2022) effective Nov 2022
key_requirements: CRITICAL: E-cigarettes regulated as tobacco products under STMA monopoly; tobacco monopoly license required for manufacturing and distribution; only STMA-approved products may be sold; flavored e-cigarettes banned for domestic consumption (only tobacco flavor allowed); CCC certification required
notes: China massive market but heavily controlled by STMA; foreign brands face near-impossible market access without STMA partnership

enforcement_body: SAMR (State Administration for Market Regulation) / STMA
key_requirements: CRITICAL: Mandatory national standard GB 41700-2022; nicotine <=20 mg/ml; child-proof design; no vitamins/caffeine additives; no prohibited substances; CCC (China Compulsory Certification) mark required; CNAS-accredited lab testing; GB standard compliance assessed by STMA-approved labs
notes: GB 41700-2022 is mandatory; non-compliant products subject to market removal; CCC mark required for all products

regulation_name: Flavored Vapes — BANNED for domestic sale
key_requirements: BANNED (flavored): All characterizing flavors in e-cigarettes prohibited for domestic Chinese market since Oct 2022; only tobacco flavor permitted; significant enforcement action; online sales of flavored vapes targeted; export products may differ but must comply if sold domestically
notes: Flavor ban complete in domestic CN market; only tobacco-flavor ENDS legal; major reformulation required for domestic sales

regulation_ref: Cap. 371 Smoking (Public Health) Ordinance + Alternative Smoking Products Ordinance 2019 effective Apr 2022
key_requirements: BANNED: Sale import manufacture and promotion of e-cigarettes (alternative smoking products) banned since April 2022; penalty HKD 50000 + 6 months imprisonment for first offence; personal possession tolerated but commercial activity fully prohibited
notes: Complete ban on commercial e-cigarettes since Apr 2022; high criminal penalties; significant enforcement

regulation_name: Tobacco Hazards Prevention Act 2023 — VAPES BANNED
regulation_ref: Tobacco Hazards Prevention Act (revised 2023 effective Jan 2024)
enforcement_body: Health Promotion Administration (HPA) / Ministry of Health and Welfare
key_requirements: BANNED: E-cigarettes prohibited under revised Tobacco Hazards Prevention Act effective 2024; all electronic cigarettes including nicotine-free classified as prohibited products; penalties up to NTD 1 million for commercial violations; heated tobacco products regulated separately
notes: Taiwan full e-cigarette ban; heated tobacco (IQOS-type) regulated separately; high penalties

regulation_ref: Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Act (PMD Act) + Tobacco Business Act
enforcement_body: MHLW (Ministry of Health Labour and Welfare) / JTS
key_requirements: BANNED (nicotine-containing): Nicotine-containing e-cigarettes classified as pharmaceutical products (unapproved); sale without pharmaceutical approval prohibited; only MHLW-approved nicotine replacement therapies permitted; nicotine-free e-liquids in grey zone; heated tobacco products (HTP) regulated under Tobacco Business Act as tobacco -- ALLOWED
notes: Japan key distinction: nicotine vapes BANNED; HTP (IQOS/glo/Ploom) fully legal under Tobacco Business Act; largest HTP market globally

regulation_name: Tobacco Business Act + Food Sanitation Act (e-liquids)
regulation_ref: Tobacco Business Act + Food Sanitation Act + National Health Promotion Act
enforcement_body: Ministry of Strategy and Finance / Ministry of Food and Drug Safety (MFDS) / MOHW
key_requirements: E-cigarettes regulated under Tobacco Business Act; nicotine e-liquids taxed as tobacco; excise + VAT + health promotion surcharge; flavor restrictions under discussion; public vaping subject to same restrictions as smoking; liquid e-cigarettes may fall under food/pharmaceutical classification depending on nicotine content
notes: KR complex multi-ministry jurisdiction; significant excise; flavor ban under legislative review 2024

regulation_ref: Tobacco (Control of Advertisements and Sale) Act (Cap. 309) + Tobacco Products Control Act amendments
key_requirements: BANNED: Sale import distribution and possession for sale of e-cigarettes prohibited under Singapore Tobacco Products Control Act; penalty up to SGD 10000 for individuals SGD 100000 for corporations; personal import also restricted; comprehensive ban strictly enforced
notes: Singapore complete ban strictly enforced; criminal penalties; no legal pathway for e-cigarette market

regulation_ref: Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act 2024 (Act 852)
key_requirements: New comprehensive act 2024 replacing previous piecemeal regulations; vaping products require product registration; nicotine e-liquids treated as tobacco products; advertising ban; age 18+; generational prohibition (tobacco and vaping products banned for those born after 2007); flavor restrictions likely under Act 852 regulations
notes: Malaysia Act 852 2024 major regulatory shift; generational prohibition unique globally; regulations under Act still being gazetted

regulation_ref: Customs Department Notification (2014) + Tobacco Product Control Act B.E. 2560 (2017)
key_requirements: BANNED: E-cigarettes banned from import export and sale since 2014 Customs notification; confirmed under Tobacco Product Control Act 2017; penalties: up to 10 years imprisonment and/or THB 500000 fine for commercial import; possession also penalised; enforcement active including tourist confiscations
notes: Thailand complete ban with criminal penalties; tourist enforcement; significant legal risk; no legal market

regulation_ref: Nghi quyet 173/NQ-CP (2024) — ban on e-cigarettes and heated tobacco products
enforcement_body: Ministry of Health Vietnam / General Department of Vietnam Customs
key_requirements: BANNED: Government Resolution 173/NQ-CP 2024 bans e-cigarettes and heated tobacco products effective 2025; full commercial and personal import ban; manufacturing prohibited; transition period for existing products; enforcement by customs and health authorities
notes: Vietnam comprehensive ban from 2025; covers both ENDS and HTP; significant market closure

regulation_ref: Undang-Undang No. 39 Tahun 2007 (Excise Law) + PMK 60/2017 + PMK update 2023
enforcement_body: Directorate General of Customs and Excise (DJBC) / Bea Cukai
key_requirements: E-cigarettes and e-liquids classified as taxable goods under excise framework; excise duty on liquid tobacco products (HPTL); excise label (banderole) required; registration of e-liquid manufacturers; minimum retail price; no outright ban; advertising restrictions apply
notes: Indonesia is large legal market; excise label compliance critical; significant excise increase planned; no flavor ban

regulation_ref: Republic Act 11900 — Vaporized Nicotine and Non-Nicotine Products Regulation Act (2022)
key_requirements: Comprehensive vape regulation Act 2022; FDA registration required; nicotine <=65 mg/ml (higher than EU limit); age 18+; advertising restrictions; public vaping regulated; flavor restrictions: FDA may limit flavors via regulations; health warnings; allows vaping as harm reduction tool
notes: Philippines RA 11900 progressive harm-reduction approach; higher nicotine limit; active FDA registration enforcement

regulation_name: PECA 2019 — Prohibition of Electronic Cigarettes Act — BANNED
regulation_ref: The Prohibition of Electronic Cigarettes (Production Manufacture Import Export Transport Sale Distribution Storage and Advertisement) Act 2019
enforcement_body: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare / State health authorities / Customs
