key_requirements: BANNED: All activities related to e-cigarettes (production manufacture import export transport sale distribution storage advertisement) prohibited under PECA 2019; imprisonment up to 3 years + fine for first offence; up to 5 years for repeat; comprehensive enforcement
notes: India complete comprehensive ban since 2019; one of strictest globally; no legal market pathway

regulation_ref: Therapeutic Goods (Standard for Nicotine Vaping Products) (TGO 110) 2021 + Therapeutic Goods Act 1989
enforcement_body: TGA (Therapeutic Goods Administration) / State health departments
key_requirements: CRITICAL: Nicotine vaping products classified as therapeutic goods; require prescription from GP; only TGA-approved products may be sold; pharmacist dispensing only; non-prescription nicotine vape sales banned; recreational vaping market prohibited; flavored vapes still available under prescription; import personal use exemption being phased out
notes: Australia unique prescription model; recreational market effectively banned; significant enforcement against illicit market; reform proposals ongoing

regulation_name: Therapeutic Goods Order TGO 110 — Nicotine Vaping Products Standards
regulation_ref: Therapeutic Goods Order No. 110 — Nicotine Vaping Products Standard 2021
key_requirements: Standards for nicotine vaping products: max nicotine 100 mg/ml (freebase); max 20 mg/ml (salt); pharmaceutical-grade ingredients; no prohibited additives; sterility requirements; TGA inclusion in ARTG required; GMP manufacturing standard
notes: TGO 110 mandates pharmaceutical standards for NVPs; significant compliance cost vs consumer products

regulation_name: Smokefree Environments and Regulated Products Act + 2023 Amendments
regulation_ref: Smokefree Environments and Regulated Products Act 1990 (amended 2022/2023) + Smokefree Environments (Regulated Products) Regulations 2021
key_requirements: Vaping products regulated; retail specialist vape store license required; age 18+; health warnings mandatory; notified product register; flavors restricted to tobacco/mint/menthol at general retailers; specialist stores may sell fruit/other flavors; public vaping aligned with smoking restrictions
notes: NZ harm-reduction approach; dual-channel retail (general vs specialist); flavor restrictions by channel type

regulation_name: ASEAN — pas de cadre harmonise ENDS
regulation_ref: N/A — ASEAN does not have harmonized e-cigarette/vaping regulation
key_requirements: No ASEAN-level harmonized framework for e-cigarettes; member states vary widely: SG/TH/VN/IN ban; MY/ID/PH allow with regulation; ASEAN Tobacco Control frameworks are non-binding; WHO FCTC guides national positions
notes: ASEAN has no bloc-level ENDS regulation; national rules dominate; included for jurisdictional completeness

regulation_ref: Saudi SFDA Circular + UAE Ministry of Health + GCC Health Ministers Council framework
enforcement_body: Saudi SFDA / UAE MOHAP / GCC Health Ministers Council
key_requirements: Varies by member state: Saudi Arabia -- SFDA regulates vaping products excise duty 100% advertising ban age 18+; UAE -- regulated and allowed with restrictions ESMA standard required excise 100%; Kuwait/Qatar -- restricted limited availability; Bahrain/Oman -- partial restrictions; no unified GCC vaping standard
notes: GCC markets differ significantly; Saudi and UAE largest markets; 100% excise duty common; ESMA/SFDA compliance required

regulation_name: Israel — Tobacco Control Law + ENDS regulations
regulation_ref: Public Health (Restrictions on Advertising and Marketing of Tobacco Products) Law 5743-1983 + Amendment 2019 covering e-cigarettes
enforcement_body: Ministry of Health Israel / Standards Institution of Israel (SII)
key_requirements: E-cigarettes regulated under tobacco framework since 2019; requires Ministry of Health registration; nicotine <=20 mg/ml (EU aligned); advertising prohibited; age 18+; public vaping restricted; health warnings; SII standard compliance required for import
notes: Israel EU-aligned approach; active enforcement; flavor restrictions under consideration 2024

regulation_name: Egypt — Ministry of Health Ban on e-cigarettes
regulation_ref: MoH Decree (Ministerial Decree on Electronic Cigarettes) + Customs Circular
enforcement_body: Egyptian Ministry of Health / Egyptian Customs Authority
key_requirements: BANNED: Ministry of Health decree prohibiting import and sale of e-cigarettes; customs enforcement at ports; limited to no formal legal market; grey market exists; decree not codified as statute but administratively enforced
notes: Egypt administrative ban; not always consistently enforced; high legal risk for commercial operations

regulation_ref: Loi n 15-91 relative a la fabrication au conditionnement et a la commercialisation des tabacs + Circulaires Ministere de la Sante
enforcement_body: Ministere de la Sante Maroc / Direction des Medicaments
key_requirements: Tobacco law Loi 15-91 covers traditional tobacco; e-cigarettes not explicitly regulated as of 2024; practical situation: vaping products sold in grey zone; Ministry of Health circulars indicate ENDS should comply with tobacco restrictions; no specific ENDS authorization framework; gap in regulation
notes: Morocco regulatory gap; ENDS in grey zone; formal registration framework lacking; market exists but regulatory risk

regulation_ref: Tobacco Products and Electronic Delivery Systems Control Bill (B29-2022)
key_requirements: Comprehensive bill covering both tobacco and electronic delivery systems (EDS); nicotine EDS regulated; registration required; advertising banned; age 18+; public vaping banned; plain packaging proposed; bill progressing through Parliament 2024; significant compliance requirements once enacted; in interim existing Tobacco Products Control Act applies
notes: ZA Bill B29-2022 comprehensive; EDS explicitly included; adoption expected 2024-2025; operators should prepare now

regulation_name: NTC Act 2015 + NAFDAC — ENDS peu reglemente
regulation_ref: National Tobacco Control Act 2015 + NAFDAC Regulations
enforcement_body: National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC) / NTCA enforcement body
key_requirements: Tobacco Control Act 2015 covers tobacco products; e-cigarettes not explicitly defined; NAFDAC has asserted authority to regulate ENDS; practical enforcement limited; growing market with regulatory uncertainty; NAFDAC product registration for any nicotine products advisable; advertising restrictions under NTC Act apply by extension
notes: Nigeria regulatory gap for ENDS; growing market; NAFDAC authority asserted but framework unclear; compliance risk

regulation_ref: Tobacco Control Act No. 4 of 2007 + Tobacco Control Regulations 2014
enforcement_body: Kenya Tobacco Control Board / Ministry of Health
key_requirements: E-cigarettes regulated under Tobacco Control Act 2007; importation requires KEBS (Kenya Bureau of Standards) pre-export verification; health warnings; advertising prohibited; public vaping banned; KEBS standard compliance required; market growing with enforcement variable
notes: Kenya KEBS compliance required for import; Tobacco Control Board enforcement increasing; East Africa market growing

regulation_ref: N/A — Codex Alimentarius covers food safety standards
key_requirements: Codex Alimentarius does not cover electronic cigarettes or vaping products; these are classified as tobacco/nicotine products outside Codex food scope; not applicable
notes: Codex not applicable to vaping/ENDS products; included for jurisdictional completeness

regulation_ref: ISO 20768:2018 (vaping terminology) + ISO 22578:2022 (e-cigarette testing) + IEC standards
enforcement_body: ISO Technical Committee TC 126 (Tobacco and tobacco products)
key_requirements: ISO 20768 provides standardized vocabulary for e-cigarettes; ISO 22578 covers aerosol chemistry testing methods; voluntary standards used by manufacturers for quality assurance and regulatory submissions; referenced in regulatory frameworks (EU Canada); not mandatory but market-facilitating
notes: ISO standards voluntary; ISO TC 126 active on ENDS; compliance improves regulatory submissions globally

regulation_ref: N/A — UNECE WP.29 covers vehicle regulations
enforcement_body: UNECE World Forum for Harmonization of Vehicle Regulations
key_requirements: UNECE WP.29 covers vehicle safety regulations; not applicable to e-cigarettes or vaping products
notes: UNECE WP.29 not applicable to ENDS; included for jurisdictional completeness

regulation_name: WADA — Nicotine non listee substance interdite
key_requirements: Nicotine is not on the WADA Prohibited List; e-cigarettes and vaping products not prohibited under anti-doping rules; however nicotine monitoring program may apply in some sports; not directly regulatory for market access
notes: WADA monitoring nicotine but not prohibited; not applicable as a market compliance barrier

regulation_name: OECD — WHO FCTC + Product Safety Guidelines
regulation_ref: WHO FCTC (Framework Convention on Tobacco Control) + OECD Consumer Product Safety Policy Guidance
key_requirements: WHO FCTC (180 parties) provides international framework for tobacco control; Article 13 advertising bans; Article 8 smoke-free environments -- FCTC guidance increasingly applied to ENDS by COP decisions; OECD guidelines on product safety; non-binding but drives national legislation globally
notes: WHO FCTC COP decisions (COP9/10) increasingly address ENDS; OECD product safety guidance referenced by national regulators

key_requirements: Successor to EC 648/2004; updated rules on surfactant biodegradability; labeling; ingredient listing; safety data sheets; applies to laundry detergent pods; key compliance instrument from entry into force
notes: Primary EU detergent regulation; check transitional provisions vs EC 648/2004

key_requirements: Surfactant biodegradability requirements; ingredient disclosure; safety data sheets; labeling requirements; still in force until Regulation 2026/405 fully applicable; pods subject to all detergent classification rules
notes: Still applicable during transitional period; ensure compliance with both 648/2004 and 2026/405

key_requirements: GHS-based classification; hazard labeling on pod packaging; SDS preparation; UFI code required; child-hazard pictograms mandatory; pods often classified Acute Tox 4 oral; strict labeling requirements on outer packaging
notes: CLP labeling on outer packaging mandatory; individual pods too small for full label but outer box must comply

key_requirements: Mandatory child-resistant packaging for liquid laundry detergent capsules (pods); child-resistant closure must comply with EN 862; container must be squeeze-and-turn or similar; specific to laundry pods due to ingestion risks; critical post-Tide Pod incidents
notes: KEY RULE: CR packaging mandatory for all liquid laundry pods in EU; EN 862 test standard applies

regulation_name: CLP Annex VIII — Poison Centre Notification
key_requirements: UFI (Unique Formula Identifier) mandatory on label; product notification to national poison centres via ECHA CPNP portal; mixture composition submission; critical for pods given high ingestion toxicity risk; deadline passed — must be compliant
notes: PCN obligation mandatory since 2021 for consumer products; UFI must appear on outer packaging

regulation_name: Biocidal Products Regulation — if antibacterial claim
key_requirements: If pod contains or claims biocidal (antibacterial/disinfecting) activity: active substance approval; product authorisation required; additional labeling; SDS; cannot claim disinfection without BPR authorisation; product type PT2/PT4
notes: Applies only if antibacterial/disinfectant claim made; many pods are pure detergents (no BPR needed)

key_requirements: Substance registration if manufactured/imported >1t/y; restriction compliance (Annex XVII); SVHC communication; SDS; surfactants and enzymes in pods subject to REACH restrictions
notes: REACH restrictions on specific surfactants and enzymes relevant to pod formulations

regulation_name: ATP — CLP Annex VI Harmonised C&L
regulation_ref: CLP Annex VI ATP updates (latest: 20th ATP)
key_requirements: Harmonised classification and labelling for specific hazardous substances in pod formulas; surfactants; enzymes; bleaching agents; must use harmonised entries where they exist
notes: Check Annex VI entries for all hazardous constituents of pod formula

key_requirements: Soluble PVA/PVOH film of pods may be considered single-use plastic packaging; SUPD restrictions; labelling marking; producer responsibility implications; currently under review for water-soluble polymers
notes: Legal uncertainty: SUPD application to PVA film pods under ongoing EC review; monitor developments

regulation_name: Réglementation détergents EU applicable en FR
key_requirements: Application directe des règlements détergents EU; étiquetage en français obligatoire; fiche de données sécurité en français; notification centre antipoison INRS/CAPTV
notes: Étiquetage FR: langue française obligatoire sur emballage extérieur

key_requirements: Contribution Citeo obligatoire; logo Triman sur emballage; point de collecte info; responsabilité élargie producteur pour emballages pods (boîte + film PVA)
notes: Citeo contribution sur chaque unité vendue en FR; Triman logo obligatoire depuis 2015

key_requirements: Affichage ingrédients produits ménagers sur site web ou QR code; applicable aux détergents; liste ingrédients accessible consommateur; renforce transparence chimique
notes: AGEC décret 2022-748 impose listing ingrédients détergents; QR code accepté

key_requirements: German Detergents Act implementing EU 648/2004; additional national requirements for ingredient labeling; biodegradability testing; data submission to UBA; German-language SDS and labeling
notes: WRMG imposes additional DE-specific reporting to UBA; Tenside register

regulation_name: EU Detergents + CLP applicable in DE
key_requirements: Direct application of EU detergent and CLP regulations; child-resistant packaging mandatory per 2015/1297; German labeling language; BAUA oversight for chemical safety
notes: German labeling language; CR packaging enforcement by market surveillance authorities

regulation_ref: UK Detergents Regulation (Retained EU Law — EC 648/2004 as amended)
key_requirements: Post-Brexit retained EU law; UK version of 648/2004 with GB-specific amendments; surfactant biodegradability; ingredient labeling; SDS in English; GB market only
notes: GB-specific UKCA marking not required for detergents but UK labeling language mandatory

regulation_ref: UK CLP (Retained EU Regulation 1272/2008 as amended by REACH etc. (Amendment) Regs 2019)
key_requirements: GHS classification; hazard labeling; SDS; UFI equivalent for GB poison centre notification; child-hazard pictograms; pods classified hazardous — strict labeling required
notes: GB UFI/poison centre notification via National Poisons Information Service (NPIS)

key_requirements: Child-resistant packaging mandatory for liquid laundry detergent capsules; same technical requirements as EU 2015/1297; EN 862 compliance; market surveillance by OPSS; high enforcement priority
notes: CR packaging mandatory in GB; OPSS active enforcement; high-priority post-incidents

key_requirements: If antibacterial/disinfectant claim: UK BPR authorisation required; active substance approval under UK scheme; separate from EU BPR post-Brexit
notes: UK BPR applies if any biocidal claim; separate GB authorisation needed

key_requirements: Swiss chemical law aligned with EU; detergents subject to ChemO; surfactant biodegradability; labeling in 3 official languages (DE/FR/IT); SDS; child protection requirements parallel to EU 2015/1297
notes: Swiss labeling: 3 official languages required; CH not in EU — separate notification needed

key_requirements: GHS-based classification and labeling aligned with EU CLP; hazard pictograms; signal words; poison centre notification to Tox Info Suisse (Zurich)
notes: Swiss poison centre: Tox Info Suisse; UFI-like notification required

key_requirements: Norway in EEA: EU REACH and detergent regulations apply via EEA agreement; Norwegian Product Control Act; biodegradability; labeling in Norwegian; child-resistant packaging per EU 2015/1297 via EEA adoption
notes: EEA member: EU detergent rules apply; Norwegian language labeling required

key_requirements: Child-resistant packaging mandatory for liquid laundry packets/pods; CPSC Regulation 16 CFR Part 1700; one of the most critical CR packaging rules in world; requires CR closure or opaque/CR container; enforcement active since 2015 rulemaking
notes: CRITICAL: CR packaging for pods mandatory; CPSC enforcement; test per ASTM F3159

key_requirements: Liquid laundry detergent packets listed as substances requiring child-resistant packaging; requires packaging that passes CPSC CR test protocols; specific to pods/liquid laundry packets; added post-incidents
notes: 16 CFR 1700.14 explicitly lists liquid laundry packets; CPSC mandatory standard

regulation_name: ASTM F3159-23 — Liquid Laundry Detergent Packets
key_requirements: Safety and performance specification for liquid laundry detergent packets; child-resistance requirements; dissolution testing; mechanical strength; film integrity; color and taste aversive requirements; labeling specifications; key industry standard
notes: ASTM F3159 is industry benchmark; CPSC may enforce via rulemaking; critical for pods

key_requirements: General performance standard for detergent packets; structural integrity; labeling; complements F3159; applies to all pod-format detergents
notes: Companion to F3159; covers structural/performance aspects beyond child-resistance

key_requirements: Labeling of hazardous household substances; pods classified hazardous (irritant/corrosive); required label statements: DANGER/WARNING; first aid; keep out of reach of children; CPSC enforcement
notes: FHSA labeling required for hazardous pods; DANGER signal word likely required

regulation_name: EPA TSCA — Toxic Substances Control Act
key_requirements: Chemical substance notification if new chemical; PMN (Pre-Manufacture Notification) for new surfactants; safer choice program if claims; existing chemicals on TSCA inventory; SDS per OSHA HCS 2012 (GHS)
notes: TSCA inventory check for all surfactants/enzymes; PMN if any new chemical not on inventory

regulation_name: Proposition 65 — CA Safe Drinking Water Act
regulation_ref: California Health & Safety Code Section 25249.5 et seq. (Prop 65)
key_requirements: Warning required if product contains listed carcinogens or reproductive toxins above safe harbor levels; relevant for surfactants; fragrances; enzyme preservatives in pods; clear and reasonable warnings
notes: Prop 65 list has 900+ chemicals; fragrances and surfactants commonly flagged; warning on label or online

regulation_name: AB 1417 — Cleaning Product Right to Know Act
key_requirements: Full ingredient disclosure for cleaning products sold in CA; website and label disclosure; fragrance ingredients; nanoparticles; intentionally added ingredients; compliance mandatory for cleaning products
notes: SB 258 + AB 1417: full ingredient list on product website; fragrance breakdown required

regulation_name: NY Household Cleansing Product Information Disclosure Program
key_requirements: Ingredient disclosure for household cleaning products sold in NY; similar to CA SB 258; full ingredient list online; fragrance components; enforced by NYSDEC
notes: NY disclosure program mirrors CA SB 258; online listing of all ingredients required

regulation_ref: SOR/2001-269 (CCCR 2001) under Canada Consumer Product Safety Act
key_requirements: Child-resistant packaging requirements for hazardous consumer chemicals; pods likely classified as Class D-1 irritant; specific container requirements; reseal child-resistant closures; testing protocols; bilingual (EN/FR) labeling mandatory
notes: CCCR 2001 is primary CR packaging rule in Canada; pods require CR closure or CR container

regulation_name: Canada Consumer Product Safety Act + Hazardous Products Act
regulation_ref: S.C. 2010, c. 21 (CCPSA) + Hazardous Products Act R.S.C. 1985
key_requirements: General product safety; hazardous product labeling per HPR/WHMIS 2015 (GHS-based); SDS for workplace products; consumer product incident reporting; pods as hazardous consumer products
notes: CCPSA requires incident reporting; HP Act/WHMIS 2015 for workplace/industrial pods

enforcement_body: Environment and Climate Change Canada + Health Canada
key_requirements: Domestic Substances List (DSL) check for all surfactants and ingredients; new substance notification if not on DSL; CEPA toxicity assessment; eco-labelling if environmental claims
notes: DSL check mandatory for all chemical ingredients; surfactants well-established but enzymes may need review

key_requirements: Official Mexican Standard for cleaning products; labeling requirements; ingredient listing; safety information; Spanish labeling; child safety warnings; precautionary statements; registration with COFEPRIS if applicable
notes: NOM-189 mandatory for cleaning products in MX; COFEPRIS oversight; pods classified as cleaning product

key_requirements: General consumer product labeling standard; Spanish language; safety warnings; manufacturer information; applies as overlay to NOM-189 for consumer products
notes: General labeling NOM applies alongside product-specific NOM-189

key_requirements: Registration or notification of household cleaning products (saneantes); pods as liquid laundry detergents require notification or registration; specific labeling in Portuguese; safety data; biodegradability; child safety warnings; ANVISA portal submission
notes: ANVISA notification/registration mandatory before Brazilian market entry; pods = saneante

key_requirements: Registration requirements for saneantes; technical files; safety and efficacy data; Portuguese labeling; importer/manufacturer establishment registration with ANVISA
notes: ANVISA registration file required; Brazilian Portuguese labeling; local responsible technical required

key_requirements: Brazilian standard for laundry detergents; technical specifications; biodegradability; performance; compliance with ABNT standards supports ANVISA registration; INMETRO certification may be required
notes: ABNT standard compliance supports ANVISA dossier; INMETRO may require conformity

enforcement_body: ANMAT (Administración Nacional de Medicamentos, Alimentos y Tecnología Médica)
key_requirements: Registration of cleaning products with ANMAT; ingredient safety; labeling in Spanish; safety warnings; biodegradability; child safety precautions; ANMAT product code required
notes: ANMAT registration required for cleaning products in AR; Spanish labeling; local importer required

regulation_name: Decreto Supremo 105/96 — Reglamento sanitario productos quimicos
enforcement_body: ISP (Instituto de Salud Pública) + SEREMI Salud
key_requirements: Sanitary registration of chemical products including cleaning agents; safety data; labeling in Spanish; ISP product registration prior to commercialisation; child safety warnings
notes: ISP registration mandatory for cleaning products in CL; pods as liquid detergents require registration

enforcement_body: SAMR (State Administration for Market Regulation) + NBS
key_requirements: Mandatory safety standard for laundry detergents; limits on harmful substances; pH; phosphate content; safety testing; labeling in Chinese (simplified); child safety warnings; GB/T standards compliance
notes: GB 19258 mandatory; Chinese labeling required; phosphate restrictions apply

regulation_name: GB/T 13174 — Laundry detergent energy efficiency labeling
key_requirements: Energy efficiency labeling for laundry detergents; low-temperature washing performance; ecological performance claims; voluntary but market-expected; pods may be subject to labeling requirements if efficiency claims made
notes: GB/T 13174 voluntary but commercially important in CN market; pods for efficiency claims

regulation_ref: Cosmetic Supervision and Administration Regulation 2020 + NMPA guidance
key_requirements: If pod has 'hand-protecting' or skin-care claims: may be classified as cosmetic under CSAR; cosmetic registration required with NMPA; dual-regulation scenario possible; most pods without cosmetic claims are not subject to CSAR
notes: Monitor labeling claims carefully; 'hand-protecting' claim triggers NMPA cosmetic regulation

regulation_ref: Measures for Environmental Management of New Chemical Substances (MEE Order 12/2020)
key_requirements: New chemical substance notification for substances not on IECSC inventory; environmental risk assessment; registration with MEE; surfactants and enzymes must be on IECSC
notes: IECSC inventory check for all ingredients; MEE notification for new substances

regulation_ref: Act No. 104 of 1962 (Household Goods Quality Labelling Act) + Detergent Labelling Regulations
key_requirements: Mandatory quality labeling for laundry detergents; ingredient categories; concentration ranges; intended use; pH; Japanese labeling language; consumer-oriented disclosure; pods must comply with labeling obligations
notes: Japanese labeling language mandatory; CAA oversight; specific detergent labeling rules

regulation_name: Industrial Safety and Health Act — chemical management
regulation_ref: Act No. 57 of 1972 (ISHL) + Ordinance on Industrial Safety and Health
enforcement_body: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare (MHLW)
key_requirements: SDS required for hazardous chemical substances; workplace chemical management; GHS labeling for industrial/professional products; for consumer pods: less direct but supply chain SDS required
notes: ISHL SDS mandatory in B2B supply chain; consumer pods less directly regulated but SDS chain required

regulation_name: APRTR Act — Pollutant Release and Transfer Register
regulation_ref: Act on Confirmation of Release Amount of Specified Chemical Substances and Promotion of Improvements in the Management Thereof (PRTR Act)
key_requirements: PRTR reporting for listed chemical substances released or transferred; applicable to manufacturers/importers of cleaning products containing PRTR-listed substances; environmental monitoring; reporting threshold-based
notes: PRTR applies if manufacturing in JP or importing >1t/y of PRTR-listed substances

regulation_name: Act on the Evaluation of Chemical Substances (Chemical Substances Control Law)
key_requirements: Pre-market assessment of chemical substances; existing chemicals on inventory; new substance notification; environmental fate and biodegradability; surfactants must be on approved list or notified
notes: Chemical Substances Control Law: check all surfactants/chemicals are pre-assessed or approved

regulation_ref: Act on Registration and Evaluation of Chemical Substances (K-REACH) No. 15164
key_requirements: Registration of chemical substances manufactured/imported >1t/y; existing substance phase-in; new substance prior registration; hazard data; environmental fate; exposure assessment; covers surfactants and all pod ingredients
notes: K-REACH deadlines passed for most substances; check registration status for all ingredients

key_requirements: Hazardous chemical substance permits; restricted substance limits; import/export permits for controlled chemicals; overlaps with K-REACH; applicable to any restricted surfactants or additives
notes: CSCA: check if any pod ingredient is on controlled/restricted substance list

regulation_name: Act on Safety Control of Children's Products
enforcement_body: KATS (Korean Agency for Technology and Standards) + MOLIT
key_requirements: Child safety products safety management; if pod marketed for household use accessible to children: child-resistant packaging requirements; KC safety confirmation mark; special safety standards for products accessible to children under 13
notes: KEY: Korea has specific children's product safety law; pods must meet KC safety confirmation requirements

regulation_ref: Product Safety Basic Act + Safety Confirmation Mark Regulations
key_requirements: KC mark (Korea Certification) required; self-certification against Korean Safety Standards; cleaning products and detergents require safety confirmation; safety test reports; labeling in Korean
notes: KC mark mandatory for cleaning products sold in KR; self-certification or third-party testing

regulation_ref: IS 4955 (BIS Standard for Synthetic Detergent Powders/Cakes)
key_requirements: BIS certification for synthetic detergents; pods as liquid laundry capsules; BIS licence required if IS standard applicable; labeling per Legal Metrology rules; safety requirements
notes: BIS IS 4955 may need extension to pod format; check applicability; BIS licence required

enforcement_body: Department for Consumer Affairs / State Legal Metrology
key_requirements: Net content declaration; MRP (Maximum Retail Price); manufacturer/importer name/address; country of origin; batch number; date of manufacture/expiry; Hindi/English labeling; MRP inclusive of all taxes
notes: Legal Metrology rules mandatory for all packaged consumer goods in IN; MRP on pack required

key_requirements: Mandatory registration/introduction scheme for industrial chemicals; surfactants and enzymes in pods require AICIS assessment; categorisation (standard/exempted/expedited/assessed); introduction certificates; replaces NICNAS from July 2020
notes: AICIS mandatory for all industrial chemical introductions; pods contain multiple chemicals needing assessment

regulation_name: ACCC Mandatory Safety Standard — Consumer Products
regulation_ref: Australian Consumer Law (Schedule 2, Competition and Consumer Act 2010) + product safety mandatory standards
key_requirements: Consumer product safety mandatory standards; general safety provision; ACCC can issue mandatory standards for detergent pods if deemed unsafe; existing product safety standards for hazardous consumer products; child safety requirements
notes: ACCC monitors pod safety; no specific mandatory standard for pods but general safety obligation applies; monitoring CR packaging

regulation_ref: Agricultural and Veterinary Chemicals Code + SUSMP (Standard for the Uniform Scheduling of Medicines and Poisons)
key_requirements: If antibacterial claim: APVMA registration required; SUSMP classification for dangerous poisons; scheduling affects sale conditions; consumer product with biocidal claim may require schedule listing
notes: Antibacterial claims trigger APVMA/SUSMP; pure detergent pods not subject to APVMA

regulation_ref: Hazardous Substances and New Organisms Act 1996 (HSNO Act)
key_requirements: Classification and approval of hazardous substances; pods likely classified as hazardous (corrosive/irritant); EPA approval or group standard compliance; labeling per HSNO; emergency response information; child-resistant packaging may be required for hazardous substances
notes: HSNO group standard for laundry products; check WGS or HPS group standard; CR packaging via HSNO controls

regulation_name: NZ Consumer Guarantees Act + Product Safety
regulation_ref: Consumer Guarantees Act 1993 + Fair Trading Act 1986 + product safety regulations
key_requirements: General product safety obligations; safe for intended use; fit for purpose; labeling accuracy; MBIE can issue product safety regulations; general consumer protection framework
notes: General safety obligation; NZ follows AU product safety framework closely

key_requirements: Gulf Standardization Organization standard for detergents; biodegradability; ingredient restrictions; labeling in Arabic; safety data; applicable across GCC member states (Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain, Oman)
notes: GSO standards mandatory in GCC; Arabic labeling required; SASO most active enforcement

key_requirements: Specific GCC standard for laundry detergents; performance requirements; labeling; safety; applicable to pod format laundry detergents; SASO conformity certification required for Saudi Arabia
notes: GSO 2530 laundry-specific standard; SASO IECEE certification for Saudi market

regulation_ref: SASO Technical Regulations + SFDA oversight for certain products
enforcement_body: SASO + SFDA (Saudi Food and Drug Authority)
key_requirements: Product conformity certification (SABER platform); SASO type approval; technical file; Arabic labeling; local sponsor/importer; safety standards compliance declaration; child safety warnings
notes: SABER platform mandatory for product entry into Saudi Arabia; conformity certificate required

enforcement_body: Ministry of Environmental Protection + National Poison Control Center
key_requirements: Classification and control of hazardous substances; pods as hazardous chemical products; permit requirements for import/manufacture; labeling in Hebrew; safety data sheets in Hebrew; poison centre notification to IPCA
notes: Israeli Hazardous Substances Law: pods likely classified hazardous; import permit may be required

key_requirements: Israeli standards for detergents; biodegradability; labeling; may require SII certification; Hebrew language labeling; child safety warnings
notes: SII certification may be required; Hebrew labeling mandatory for IL market

regulation_ref: VC 8054 or equivalent NRCS compulsory specification for cleaning agents
key_requirements: South African compulsory specification for cleaning agents/household products; safety testing; labeling requirements; child safety; product registration or Letter of Authority (LoA) from NRCS; biodegradability
notes: NRCS LoA required; compulsory specification compliance mandatory; safety labeling in English

regulation_ref: Occupational Health and Safety Act 85/1993 — HCS Regulations
key_requirements: Hazardous chemical substances management in workplace supply chain; SDS required; GHS-aligned labeling; occupational health requirements for manufacturing and distribution
notes: HCS regulations for supply chain; SDS in English required for ZA market

regulation_ref: Technical Regulation TR TS 008/2011 (Consumer Goods Safety in EAEU)
enforcement_body: Federal Service for Accreditation (Rosaccreditation) + Eurasian Economic Commission
key_requirements: Mandatory certification for consumer goods in EAEU (Russia, Belarus, Kazakhstan, Armenia, Kyrgyzstan); EAC mark; conformity assessment; labeling in Russian; safety requirements; detergent pods as consumer goods
notes: EAC mark mandatory; TR TS 008/2011 covers consumer goods; Russian labeling required

regulation_ref: TR EAEU 014/2011 or equivalent chemical product technical regulation
key_requirements: Chemical product safety requirements in EAEU; SDS; labeling; classification; packaging requirements; applicable to detergent formulations; EAC certification
notes: TR EAEU chemical regulation applies alongside TR TS 008; check current EAEU regulation numbering

regulation_ref: GOST R 25644-96 (laundry detergent powder) + GOST standards for detergents
key_requirements: Russian GOST standards for detergents; safety; labeling requirements; Russian language mandatory; EAC conformity certificate for EAEU; Rospotrebnadzor sanitary registration may be required
notes: GOST R compliance + sanitary registration (Rospotrebnadzor) for chemical products in Russia

regulation_ref: Deterjan ve Deterjanlarda Kullanılan Yüzey Aktif Maddelere İlişkin Yönetmelik (aligned with EU 648/2004)
enforcement_body: Çevre, Şehircilik ve İklim Değişikliği Bakanlığı (Ministry of Environment)
key_requirements: Turkish detergent regulation aligned with EU 648/2004; biodegradability; ingredient labeling in Turkish; SDS in Turkish; market surveillance; applicable to laundry detergent pods
notes: Turkish regulation mirrors EU 648/2004; Turkish language labeling required; Ministry of Environment oversight

regulation_ref: Turkish Chemicals Legislation (aligned with EU REACH/CLP via Customs Union)
key_requirements: GHS-based classification and labeling aligned with EU CLP via Customs Union agreement; Turkish SDS; hazard communication in Turkish; child safety warnings
notes: TR aligns with EU CLP via Customs Union; Turkish language SDS and labeling required

key_requirements: Environmental management system standard; voluntary certification; relevant for manufacturing operations; supports eco-credentials and regulatory compliance across all markets; demonstrates environmental commitment
notes: ISO 14001 voluntary EMS standard; supports market access in EU/AU/CA with eco-procurement requirements

key_requirements: Quality management system; voluntary; supports product consistency and regulatory compliance globally; often required by retailer and procurement frameworks; relevant for pod manufacturing quality control
notes: ISO 9001 voluntary QMS; commercially expected by major retailers globally

regulation_name: EU Detergents Regulation — directly applicable in Italy
key_requirements: EU regulation directly applicable in Italy; surfactant biodegradability; labeling in Italian; SDS; pods must include child-safety warnings; REACH/CLP obligations apply
notes: EU regulation directly applicable in IT; Italian labeling language required per Annex VII

key_requirements: Registration and restriction of chemical substances; SVHC communication; pods contain surfactants subject to REACH registration; no separate Italian national law
notes: Italy enforces REACH via national competent authority; no additional national rules

regulation_name: EU Detergents Regulation — directly applicable in Spain
key_requirements: EU regulation directly applicable in Spain; labeling in Spanish mandatory; surfactant biodegradability; ingredient disclosure; SDS; child-safety closure required for pods
notes: EU regulation directly applicable in ES; Spanish labeling language required

key_requirements: GHS classification and labeling; UFI mandatory; Spanish-language hazard statements; pods classified as hazardous; child-resistant packaging mandatory
notes: Spain enforces CLP nationally; pods must meet child-resistant packaging Regulation (EU) 2023/1642

regulation_name: EU Detergents Regulation — directly applicable in NL
key_requirements: EU regulation directly applicable in Netherlands; Dutch labeling language required; surfactant biodegradability; ingredient disclosure; SDS; child-safety closure for pods
notes: EU regulation directly applicable in NL; Dutch labeling required; RIVM active enforcer

key_requirements: REACH substance registration; SVHC communication; no additional NL national chemicals law for detergents
notes: Netherlands among strictest REACH enforcers; monitor Dutch RIVM guidance

enforcement_body: State Service of Ukraine on Food Safety and Consumer Protection (SSUFSCP)
key_requirements: Registration of hazardous chemicals; labeling in Ukrainian; SDS in Ukrainian; pods classified as hazardous household chemicals; notification required
notes: Ukraine not in EU; own chemicals framework; Cabinet of Ministers Resolution 1156 on dangerous substances labeling

regulation_name: Ukraine Technical Regulation on Household Chemicals
regulation_ref: Resolution of Cabinet of Ministers of Ukraine No. 156 (2013)
key_requirements: Conformity assessment for household chemical products; labeling requirements; composition disclosure; Ukrainian language mandatory on packaging
notes: Primary UA regulation for detergent pods; requires Ukrainian-language full label

regulation_name: Public Health and Municipal Services Ordinance (Cap. 132)
enforcement_body: Food and Environmental Hygiene Department (FEHD) / Customs and Excise
key_requirements: Household chemical products must be safe for use; labeling in Chinese (Traditional) and English; no specific pod regulation but general consumer safety applies; import notification for hazardous chemicals
notes: HK has no standalone detergent regulation; PHMSO + general consumer safety law applies

regulation_name: Chemical Weapons (Convention) Implementation Ordinance — export controls
enforcement_body: Hong Kong Customs and Excise Department
key_requirements: Certain chemical precursors subject to export control; applicable to supply chain documentation for pod ingredients
notes: Precautionary: verify no scheduled chemicals in pod formula per Cap. 578 schedules

regulation_name: Toxic and Chemical Substances Control Act (TCSCA)
key_requirements: Registration of toxic chemical substances; hazard classification; labeling in Traditional Chinese; SDS; pods may contain substances on TCSCA list; manufacturer/importer registration required
notes: Taiwan has standalone toxic chemicals act; verify surfactants and biocide ingredients vs Schedule 1/2/3 lists

regulation_ref: Consumer Protection Act (消費者保護法)
key_requirements: Chinese-language labeling mandatory; ingredient disclosure for hazardous products; origin marking; net quantity; importer contact
notes: Labeling in Traditional Chinese required; pods must include child-safety warnings under TFDA guidance

regulation_name: Environmental Protection and Management Act (EPMA) — Hazardous Substances
key_requirements: Schedule 1 hazardous substances registration; import/export license; SDS required; pods may contain controlled surfactants or biocides requiring NEA registration
notes: SG requires NEA license for hazardous substances; verify pod ingredients vs EPMA Schedule 1

key_requirements: Consumer goods safety; no specific pod standard under CPSR currently but general safety obligation applies
notes: CPSR covers listed consumer goods; detergent pods not currently listed but monitor for additions

key_requirements: Classification of hazardous chemical waste; SDS; pods ingredients that are scheduled wastes must be managed per SW regulations; importer registration
notes: Malaysia: EQA covers chemicals disposal; no standalone detergent law; consumer protection via Trade Descriptions Act

regulation_name: Control of Industrial Major Accident Hazards (CIMAH) Regulations + Trade Descriptions Act
enforcement_body: Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living / DOSH
key_requirements: Labeling accuracy; Malay and English labeling acceptable; ingredient disclosure; false/misleading claims prohibited; CIMAH applies if manufacturing site
notes: Malay-language labeling required for domestic market; pods must disclose active ingredients

enforcement_body: Food and Drug Administration Thailand (Thai FDA) / Industrial Works Dept
key_requirements: Registration of hazardous household chemicals; pods likely Type 2 or 3 hazardous substance; labeling in Thai; SDS; registration number on packaging
notes: Thai HSA is primary framework; pods with biocide component require Type 3 registration with Thai FDA

enforcement_body: Ministry of Industry and Trade (MOIT) Vietnam
key_requirements: Chemical declaration; classification and labeling in Vietnamese; SDS in Vietnamese; hazardous household chemicals subject to declaration and annual reporting; pods require compliance with Annex labeling
notes: Vietnam: Decree 113 is primary chemicals law; pods classified as hazardous household chemical; declaration to MOIT required

regulation_name: Regulation of Minister of Trade (Permendag) on Product Labeling
key_requirements: Indonesian-language labeling mandatory; ingredient disclosure; net content; origin; importer info; halal certification if marketed as halal; pods with chemical ingredients may require BPOM notification
notes: Indonesia: Bahasa Indonesia labeling mandatory; BPOM involved for household chemicals; halal logo if claimed

regulation_name: Government Regulation on Management of Hazardous Substances (B3)
regulation_ref: PP No. 74/2001 on Management of Hazardous and Toxic Materials
key_requirements: B3 registration for hazardous chemicals; SDS; transport labeling; detergent pods with biocide classified as B3 household hazardous waste
notes: B3 regulation applies to end-of-life and transport; importer must register hazardous substances

regulation_name: RA 6969 — Toxic Substances and Hazardous and Nuclear Wastes Control Act
regulation_ref: Republic Act No. 6969 / DENR DAO 29 Series 1992
enforcement_body: Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR) — EMB
key_requirements: Chemical registration under Priority Chemical List (PCL); SDS; labeling; pods with biocide/surfactant may be PCL-listed; importer chemical registration required
notes: RA 6969 is primary PH chemicals law; DENR EMB administers PCL; pods require pre-importation clearance if PCL substance

regulation_name: ASEAN Harmonized Regulatory Framework for Household Hazardous Chemicals
regulation_ref: ASEAN Sectoral MRA — Chemical Sector (Household Chemicals Annex)
key_requirements: Mutual recognition principles for conformity assessment; SDS harmonization across ASEAN; GHS-aligned labeling; member states apply national regulations but MRA facilitates market access
notes: ASEAN MRA does not replace national law; provides facilitation for compliant products; verify each MS separately

regulation_name: GMC Resolution on Household Chemical Products Labeling
regulation_ref: GMC Resolution No. 36/1996 (Labeling of Household Chemical Products)
enforcement_body: MERCOSUR Common Market Group / national agencies (ANMAT-AR; INMETRO-BR; INSP-UY; INAN-PY)
key_requirements: Common labeling requirements for household chemical products in MERCOSUR zone; ingredient listing; hazard symbols; child-safety warnings; language requirements per member state
notes: MERCOSUR GMC Res 36/1996 harmonizes detergent labeling; individual MS may have additional rules (see AR

regulation_name: Decreto 1545/1998 — Productos de Aseo Higiene y Limpieza
enforcement_body: INVIMA (Instituto Nacional de Vigilancia de Medicamentos y Alimentos)
key_requirements: Registro sanitario (sanitary registration) mandatory for household cleaning products; labeling in Spanish; ingredient disclosure; child-safety closure for pods; net content; manufacturer/importer info
notes: Colombia: INVIMA sanitary registration required before commercialization; pods classified as household cleaning product

regulation_ref: EOS Standard ES 2500 / Chemical Products Standards
enforcement_body: Egyptian Organization for Standardization and Quality (EOS)
key_requirements: Type approval and conformity certificate for imported detergents; Arabic labeling mandatory; ingredient disclosure; SDS; EOS conformity certificate at customs; pods must meet physical-chemical requirements
notes: Egypt: EOS certification required for import; Arabic label mandatory; border inspection by GOEIC (General Organization for Export and Import Control)

regulation_name: Décret n° 2-12-198 relatif aux produits chimiques dangereux
enforcement_body: Ministère de l'Industrie et du Commerce / ONSSA
key_requirements: Classification et étiquetage des produits chimiques dangereux; Arabic+French labeling; SDS en français/arabe; enregistrement auprès du Ministère; les pods classés produit chimique dangereux
notes: Maroc: décret 2-12-198 cadre primaire; étiquetage bilingue arabe/français obligatoire; enregistrement importer

regulation_name: NAFDAC Regulation on Household Chemicals and Cleaning Products
regulation_ref: NAFDAC Regulation 2019 / Food and Drugs Act Cap F33
enforcement_body: National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC)
key_requirements: NAFDAC product registration mandatory for household cleaning products including laundry pods; labeling in English; ingredient disclosure; manufacturer and importer info; safety warnings; child-resistant closure
notes: Nigeria: NAFDAC registration is prerequisite for import and sale; registration valid 5 years; local responsible agent required

regulation_name: Standards Act Cap 496 — KEBS Compulsory Standards + PVoC
regulation_ref: Kenya Bureau of Standards (KEBS) PVoC Programme
key_requirements: Pre-Export Verification of Conformity (PVoC) required for imported consumer goods including detergents; conformity to applicable KS standard; certificate of conformity before shipment; labeling in English
notes: Kenya PVoC mandatory for imports; KEBS issues Certificate of Conformity at origin; detergent pods require inspection pre-shipment

regulation_name: OECD HPV Chemicals Programme — High Production Volume Chemicals Assessment
key_requirements: Hazard assessment of HPV chemicals including surfactants in detergent pods; OECD test guidelines (TG 301/302/306) for biodegradability; SIDS initial assessment; data sharing obligations for producers
notes: OECD soft-law applicable to formulation and safety chemical assessment; no binding enforcement but referenced by REACH/EPA/TCSA

regulation_name: WADA Prohibited List — Not Applicable to Detergent Products
key_requirements: WADA Prohibited List governs doping in sport; not applicable to household laundry detergent pods; no prohibited substances relevant; jurisdiction included for completeness
notes: Not applicable: WADA regulates doping in sport; laundry pods have no relevance to WADA framework

regulation_name: UNECE GHS — Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labelling
enforcement_body: United Nations Economic Commission for Europe (UNECE)
key_requirements: GHS underpins classification and labeling systems worldwide; pods typically classified as Acute Tox 4 (oral) + Skin Corr 1; recommended labeling elements; basis for EU CLP and many national GHS implementations
notes: UNECE GHS is soft-law framework; binding implementation via national regulations (CLP in EU; HazCom in US; etc.); relevant to pod label design globally

regulation_name: Codex Alimentarius — Not Applicable to Laundry Detergent Pods
key_requirements: Codex standards govern food safety; not applicable to laundry detergent pods; no Codex standard covers household cleaning products; jurisdiction included for completeness
notes: Not applicable: Codex covers food/feed; laundry pods are non-food chemical products outside Codex scope

key_requirements: Harmonisation des listes de vitamines/minéraux autorisés (Annexe I); formes sources autorisées (Annexe II); limites d'UL à définir par États membres; notification avant mise sur marché selon droit national
notes: Directive laisse marge nationale sur LMS (upper limits); liste positive vitamines/minéraux; base réglementaire principale EU

key_requirements: Substances ajoutées aux aliments (fortification); listes Annexe I (autorisées) / Annexe III (restrictions/interdictions); compléments alimentaires dans son champ quand ajout volontaire; sources pureté requise
notes: Complète Directive 2002/46; s'applique si vitamines/minéraux ajoutés hors liste ou avec restrictions

regulation_name: Règlement allégations nutritionnelles et de santé
key_requirements: Allégations nutritionnelles (ex. «riche en vitamine C»): conditions spécifiques; allégations de santé: liste positive Article 13 (Règl. 432/2012); allégations réduction de risque: Article 14; interdiction allégations médicales
notes: Registre EU d'allégations de santé autorisées obligatoire; allégations non listées = interdites

key_requirements: Étiquetage obligatoire: dénomination «complément alimentaire»; dose journalière recommandée (DJR); avertissement «ne pas dépasser la dose journalière»; avertissement «tenir hors de portée des enfants»; valeur nutritive si allégation
notes: FIC s'applique aux compléments alimentaires; tableau nutritionnel optionnel sauf allégation

key_requirements: Régit aliments pour nourrissons/enfants/sportifs; compléments alimentaires destinés à ces groupes doivent respecter prescriptions; ne pas faire de claims «pour sportifs» si non conforme
notes: Pertinent si le complément cible sportifs ou enfants; exigences supplémentaires

regulation_ref: EFSA Scientific Opinions on Tolerable Upper Intake Levels
key_requirements: EFSA établit les UL (Tolerable Upper Intake Levels) par nutriment; base pour fixation des LMS nationales; avis disponibles pour vitamines A/D/E/K/B6/B12/C/folate/iode/zinc/fer/calcium/magnésium etc.
notes: Pas de règlement unique EU fixant les LMS; États membres s'appuient sur EFSA UL pour fixer leurs limites nationales

key_requirements: Notification obligatoire DGCCRF via Téléicare avant mise sur marché; liste positive substances actives; étiquetage obligatoire: DJR/VNR%; avertissement surdosage; forme galénique (gélule/comprimé/poudre); interdiction claims médicaux
notes: Notification via Téléicare (plateforme DGCCRF) = spécificité française; délai 2 mois avant commercialisation; ANSES peut émettre avis risque

regulation_name: Arrêté vitamines minéraux France — formes et teneurs
key_requirements: Liste des vitamines et minéraux autorisés; formes chimiques sources autorisées; teneurs maximales par DJR fixées par arrêté (ex. vitamine D3 max 50 µg/j); valeurs de référence VNR
notes: Arrêté plus restrictif que Directive UE sur certains nutrients (ex. vit.D); vérifier arrêtés modificatifs récents (2023)

key_requirements: Notification obligatoire BVL avant première mise sur marché en Allemagne; liste positive vitamines/minéraux + sources (Annexe 1); LMS fixées par Allemagne (BfR recommandations); étiquetage: DJR VNR%; avertissement enfants/grossesse
notes: BfR a émis des recommandations de LMS qui de facto s'imposent; notification en ligne BVL; amende jusqu'à 50 000 € si non déclaré

key_requirements: Notification obligatoire Ministero della Salute via portail; liste vitamines/minéraux + formes + teneurs (liste nationale + UE); étiquetage en italien; dose journalière; avertissement dépassement
notes: Italie maintient liste nationale spécifique; Ministero della Salute publie tableau actualisé des substances autorisées; notification très rapide (ack immédiat)

regulation_name: Notification Ministero della Salute — procédure en ligne
key_requirements: Soumission notification en ligne via PPA; paiement droit d'enregistrement; attribution numéro d'autorisation avant commercialisation; modification produit = nouvelle notification
notes: Système PPA en ligne; délai traitement ~ 30 jours; certification traducteur si documents étrangers

enforcement_body: AESAN (Agencia Española de Seguridad Alimentaria y Nutrición)
key_requirements: Notification obligatoire AESAN avant commercialisation; liste substances autorisées (vitamines/minéraux) + formes + LMS; étiquetage en espagnol: DJR; mentions obligatoires; interdiction claims thérapeutiques
notes: AESAN maintient registre public des compléments notifiés; nouvelles substances = avis AESAN + approbation AESAN/MSPSI

key_requirements: Notification via NVWA avant mise sur marché; LMS fixées par arrêté néerlandais (Tableau 1 valeurs max/DJR); liste formes autorisées; étiquetage: DJR; mentions de sécurité
notes: Pays-Bas = LMS parmi les plus restrictives d'UE; revues suite Gezondheidsraad; KAG (Keuringsraad) vérifie allégations publicité

regulation_ref: Food Supplements (England) Regulations 2003 (SI 2003/1387) + equivalents Écosse/Galles/Irlande du Nord
key_requirements: Post-Brexit: maintient listes de vitamines/minéraux + formes (Annexe I/II équivalent); notification non requise au niveau UK (pas de système centralisé); étiquetage UK: mentions obligatoires, DJR UK RNI/DRV; interdiction claims médicaux
notes: Post-Brexit: UK conserve le cadre 2003; DHSC/FSA envisage révision des UL UK; pas de notification centralisée UK mais étiquetage en anglais UK obligatoire

regulation_name: UK FSA — Novel Food et NDI UK
regulation_ref: UK Novel Foods Regulations 2021 (retained EU law amended)
key_requirements: Nouveaux ingrédients non sur marché avant 1997 = Novel Food notification UK obligatoire; procédure séparée de celle EU post-Brexit; GB (pas NI) a son propre régime; ingrédients botanique = zone grise
notes: NI reste alignée sur règles UE (Protocol NI); GB = règles UK propres; bifidum/certains extraits végétaux peuvent nécessiter Novel Food GB

regulation_ref: Ordonnance du DFI sur les compléments alimentaires (RS 817.022.32)
key_requirements: Notification obligatoire OSAV avant mise sur marché CH; liste vitamines/minéraux + formes (Annexe 1 OCA); LMS CH fixées (tableau OSAV); étiquetage en allemand/français/italien selon région; DJR; mentions sécurité; interdiction claims médicaux
notes: CH droit alimentaire aligné sur UE mais autonome; OCA révisée 2016; OSAV = Bundesamt für Lebensmittelsicherheit und Veterinärwesen (BLV); délai notification ~ 60 j

regulation_ref: Forskrift om kosttilskudd av 20. mai 2004 (dernière modif. 2023)
key_requirements: Notification obligatoire Mattilsynet avant mise sur marché; liste vitamines/minéraux autorisés alignée sur Directive 2002/46 (EEA); LMS norvégiennes (avis VKM); étiquetage en norvégien; DJR; avertissements surdosage
notes: Norvège = EEA; Directive 2002/46 s'applique via EEA Agreement; VKM (Vitenskapskomiteen) établit UL; Mattilsynet maintient registre

regulation_name: Règlement technique TR TS 021/2011 sécurité alimentaire (EAEU)
key_requirements: Compléments alimentaires = produits alimentaires spécialisés en droit EAEU; TR TS 021 = exigences sécurité générale; dossier de conformité + certificat TR TS ou déclaration de conformité; contrôles composition; interdiction claims médicaux
notes: Russie dans EAEU; compléments alimentaires suivent régime aliment spécialisé; enregistrement d'état obligatoire (Госрегистрация) via Rospotrebnadzor avant commercialisation

regulation_ref: Порядок государственной регистрации специализированных продуктов (TP TC 027/2012 + РФ-specific)
key_requirements: Enregistrement obligatoire compléments alimentaires avant importation/vente RU; dossier: composition, sécurité, efficacité; validité 5 ans; étiquetage en russe obligatoire; interdit: claims thérapeutiques; LMS selon normes nationales
notes: Procédure longue (3-6 mois); documents doivent être traduits en russe; notarisation requise; dépôt via portail Gosuslugi ou directement Rospotrebnadzor

regulation_ref: TR TS 027/2012 (Technical Regulation on Specialized Food Products)
key_requirements: Régit les aliments spécialisés y compris compléments alimentaires; harmonise exigences entre RU/BY/KZ/AM/KG; liste substances autorisées; exigences sécurité; étiquetage; certification EAC obligatoire pour commercialisation dans l'espace EAEU
notes: TR TS 027 complète TR TS 021; marque EAC obligatoire; enregistrement d'état commun utilisable dans tous les pays EAEU

regulation_ref: Takviye Edici Gıdalar Yönetmeliği (Official Gazette 16.08.2013 / modifiée 2023)
enforcement_body: TÜRKAK / Tarım ve Orman Bakanlığı (Ministère de l'Agriculture)
key_requirements: Notification obligatoire avant mise sur marché; liste positive vitamines/minéraux autorisés; LMS fixées par règlement (Ek-1); formes chimiques autorisées; étiquetage en turc; DJR; mentions obligatoires sécurité; interdiction claims thérapeutiques
notes: Turquie règlement inspiré du modèle UE; TÜRKAK accrédite laboratoires de contrôle; importations nécessitent certificat santé pays d'origine

enforcement_body: Centre d'expertise médicale nationale / ДПСС (Service sanitaire)
key_requirements: Compléments alimentaires = catégorie alimentaire spéciale; enregistrement d'état obligatoire; dossier sécurité; composition selon liste autorisée; étiquetage en ukrainien; DJR; avertissements; interdiction claims médicaux
notes: Guerre a perturbé certains processus; enregistrement peut prendre 4-6 mois; reconnaissance mutuelle avec UE en discussion (accord DCFTA)

regulation_name: DSHEA 1994 — Dietary Supplement Health and Education Act
regulation_ref: Dietary Supplement Health and Education Act of 1994 (PL 103-417)
enforcement_body: FDA (CFSAN — Center for Food Safety and Applied Nutrition)
key_requirements: Régime food aux US (pas OTC); fabricant responsable de la sécurité avant mise sur marché; pas de pré-approbation FDA requis; notification Structure/Function Claim 30j avant commercialisation; etiquetage: Supplement Facts Panel; identité; pureté; force; composition
notes: DSHEA = régime favorable industry; FDA peut agir post-market si risque; compléments ≠ médicaments; pas de DIN/NPN requis aux US

regulation_ref: 21 CFR Part 111 (Current Good Manufacturing Practice in Manufacturing/Packaging/Labeling/Holding for Dietary Supplements)
key_requirements: GMP obligatoires pour tous fabricants/emballeurs/étiqueteurs/importateurs; contrôle qualité: tests identité/pureté/force/composition; Master Manufacturing Records; registres lot; vérification fournisseurs; gestion non-conformités; audits périodiques
notes: Inspections FDA fréquentes; 483 observations courantes: manque contrôle identité matières premières; défauts MBR; importateurs = soumis mêmes GMP

key_requirements: Ingrédient non commercialisé avant 15 oct. 1994 = New Dietary Ingredient; notification à FDA 75 jours avant commercialisation; dossier: identité, conditions d'utilisation, historique sécurité, études; FDA peut répondre par lettre de refus ou silence (accepté)
notes: Nombreux ingrédients botaniques ou extraits = potentiellement NDI; règle souvent négligée = risque warning letter FDA; DSSA 2022 cherche à renforcer

key_requirements: Allégation structure/fonction (ex. «la vitamine C contribue au soutien du système immunitaire»): notification à FDA dans les 30 jours post-commercialisation; étiquetage: disclaimer obligatoire «This statement has not been evaluated by the FDA. Not intended to diagnose...»; interdiction claims maladie
notes: Distinction S/F claim vs disease claim critique; claim maladie = reclassification médicament; FTC supervise publicité claims

regulation_name: DSSA — Dietary Supplement Listing Modernization Act (proposé)
regulation_ref: S. 4090 / HR 7529 (118th Congress — proposé mais non encore adopté en 2026)
key_requirements: Proposition d'un registre obligatoire des compléments alimentaires auprès de la FDA; liste de produits et ingrédients; si adopté: délai d'enregistrement 6 mois après promulgation; non encore loi en 2026 — à surveiller
notes: Législation en cours au Congrès; FDA favorable; industrie partiellement opposée; si adopté avant fin 2026 = compliance action requise immédiatement

regulation_ref: California Proposition 65 (Safe Drinking Water and Toxic Enforcement Act of 1986)
enforcement_body: OEHHA (Office of Environmental Health Hazard Assessment)
key_requirements: Certaines vitamines à haute dose ou impuretés potentielles (ex. vitamine A > seuil = teratogène; fer; plomb dans extraits végétaux) déclenchent obligation d'avertissement Prop 65; seuil NSRL/MADL par substance; safe harbor labels requis si dépassement
notes: Prop 65 = source litiges (60-day notice); tester traces plomb/arsenic dans compléments; vitamine A forme rétinol > 10 000 UI/j peut nécessiter avertissement; fish oil = mercure possible

regulation_name: New York Education Law §6814 — Age restriction supplements
key_requirements: Interdiction de vente de compléments alimentaires contenant des stimulants (caféine > seuil; éphédrine; yohimbine; etc.) aux mineurs de moins de 18 ans en New York; obligation de demander preuve d'âge; applicables aux détaillants physiques et en ligne (pour livraisons NY)
notes: Vitamines classiques non concernées si sans stimulants; complexes B + caféine ou pré-workout = vérifier composition; amendes retailer possible

regulation_name: Règlement sur les produits de santé naturels (SOR/2003-196) + NPN
enforcement_body: Health Canada — Direction des produits de santé naturels et sans ordonnance (DPSN)
key_requirements: Régime Natural Health Product (NHP) — plus strict que US; Numéro de Produit Naturel (NPN) obligatoire avant vente; pré-approbation Health Canada requise; dossier: ingrédients médicinaux + non médicinaux; allégations (claims) pré-approuvées; GMP certificat obligatoire; site de fabrication accrédité
notes: Canada = régime hybride food/drug; compléments alimentaires = NHP (pas food); NPN process: 12-24 mois; reformulation = nouvelle demande; étiquetage bilingue FR/EN obligatoire

regulation_ref: Acuerdo por el que se determinan los lineamientos para la autorización de productos de complementación alimentaria (COFEPRIS 2010) + NOM-051-SCFI/SSA1-2010
enforcement_body: COFEPRIS (Comisión Federal para la Protección contra Riesgos Sanitarios)
key_requirements: Compléments alimentaires = «productos de complementación alimentaria»; enregistrement sanitaire COFEPRIS obligatoire (ISAN); dossier: composition; fabricant; contrôle qualité; étiquetage selon NOM-051; allégations autorisées limitées; interdiction claims thérapeutiques; GMP requis
notes: COFEPRIS enregistrement long (6-18 mois); renouvellement tous les 5 ans; importation = agent légal au Mexique + COFEPRIS permit; NOM-051 = étiquetage général aliments + compléments

key_requirements: Compléments alimentaires = catégorie réglementée ANVISA; notification obligatoire via DATAVISA avant commercialisation; liste substances autorisées (Annexe I RDC 243); LMS; formes galéniques autorisées; étiquetage en portugais; liste d'allégations autorisées RDC 459
notes: Brésil a simplifié le régime en 2018 (notification vs enregistrement complet); ANVISA maintient liste positive actualisée; substances non listées = interdites par défaut

regulation_ref: CAA Resolución Conjunta SPyRS y SAGPyA N° 1294/97 + actualizaciones ANMAT
enforcement_body: ANMAT (Administración Nacional de Medicamentos Alimentos y Tecnología Médica)
key_requirements: Compléments alimentaires = «suplementos dietarios» dans le CAA; enregistrement ANMAT obligatoire (RNPA); liste positive vitamines/minéraux + formes; LMS; étiquetage en espagnol; DJR; mentions obligatoires; certificat GMP exigé
notes: ANMAT a mis à jour le régime en 2020-2023; classification peut basculer vers médicament si claim thérapeutique; procédure ANMAT longue (12-24 mois); agent local requis

regulation_name: Reglamento Sanitario Alimentos DS 977/96 — compléments Chili
regulation_ref: DS N° 977/96 (Reglamento Sanitario de los Alimentos) Título XX Suplementos alimenticios
enforcement_body: SEREMI de Salud / ISP (Instituto de Salud Pública)
key_requirements: Suplementos alimenticios = catégorie alimentaire; notification ISP obligatoire avant commercialisation; liste vitamines/minéraux + LMS; étiquetage en espagnol; DJR; avertissements; interdiction claims thérapeutiques; GMP recommandés mais non obligatoires à ce niveau
notes: Chili: loi 20.606 (étiquetage nutritionnel) peut impacter présentation; ISP vérifie conformité; importation = certificat d'origine + analyse lot

regulation_ref: Resolución 3096 de 2007 (Ministerio de la Protección Social)
enforcement_body: INVIMA (Instituto Nacional de Vigilancia de Medicamentos y Alimentos)
key_requirements: Compléments alimentaires = «suplementos dietarios»; enregistrement INVIMA requis (RSA/NSA); liste substances actives; LMS; formes autorisées; étiquetage en espagnol; DJR; allégations limitées; GMP requis; importation: agente aduanero + INVIMA permit
notes: INVIMA enregistrement: 6-18 mois; renouvellement périodique; vigilancia post-marché; publicité contrôlée par INVIMA

regulation_ref: GMC Resolución N° 18/02 (Alimentos para propósitos especiales — Suplementos Dietarios)
key_requirements: Harmonisation régionale des compléments alimentaires (Argentine/Brésil/Paraguay/Uruguay/Venezuela); LMS harmonisées; substances autorisées; étiquetage harmonisé; base pour règlements nationaux membres
notes: MERCOSUR fournit le cadre; application nationale via ANMAT (AR) / ANVISA (BR); Paraguay/Uruguay ont implémenté avec variantes locales

regulation_ref: 健康食品注册管理办法 (Order 23/2016) + Health Food Registration Measures
key_requirements: Régime Health Food (健康食品) = le plus strict; enregistrement NMPA obligatoire avant commercialisation; «Blue Hat» (蓝帽子) label; dossier: formule; procédé; efficacité; toxicité; études cliniques potentielles; allégations de santé pré-approuvées (28 fonctions); étiquetage en chinois simplifié; LMS strictes
notes: Enregistrement NMPA = 2-4 ans; coût élevé; importation nécessite licence import chine + Health Food enregistrement; alternative: régime «filing» pour compléments nutriment pur

regulation_name: Standard national GB 16740 Health Foods Chine
enforcement_body: NMPA + SAMR (State Administration for Market Regulation)
key_requirements: Standard national obligatoire pour Health Foods; définit catégories; exigences qualité/sécurité; paramètres physicochimiques; microbiologie; métaux lourds; résidus; étiquetage; base pour évaluation NMPA
notes: GB 16740 complète le cadre réglementaire; fabricants étrangers = produits testés selon GB standards; laboratoire accrédité CNAS requis

regulation_name: Régime Filing compléments nutritionnels simples Chine
regulation_ref: Filing system for Nutrient Supplements (营养素补充剂备案制) — Announcement 2016
key_requirements: Pour compléments à base de vitamines/minéraux purs (sans fonctions de santé allégées): régime de filing (déclaration) simplifié vs enregistrement complet; liste fermée de substances + LMS; pas d'allégation de santé autorisée sous ce régime
notes: Filing = 3-6 mois vs 2-4 ans enregistrement; mais zéro allégation de santé; si allégation = enregistrement obligatoire; vitamines de base (A/D/E/K/B/C) et minéraux éligibles au filing

regulation_name: PHMSO + Registration pharmaceutique si claim — Hong Kong
regulation_ref: Public Health and Municipal Services Ordinance (Cap. 132) + Pharmacy and Poisons Ordinance (Cap. 138)
enforcement_body: FEHD (Food and Environmental Hygiene Department) + Pharmacy and Poisons Board
key_requirements: Compléments vitamines sans claim médical = food; pas de pré-enregistrement aliment requis; mais si claim thérapeutique/médicale = enregistrement comme médicament sous Cap. 138; étiquetage: identité; contenu; avertissements; importation sans notification spéciale si food
notes: HK = porte d'entrée Chine continentale; wording des claims crucial: food claim (Cap.132) vs drug claim (Cap.138); HK FSC publie guidelines sur allégations

regulation_name: Food Safety Act + Health Food Control Act Taiwan
regulation_ref: Loi sur la sécurité alimentaire (食品安全衛生管理法) + Loi sur les aliments de santé (健康食品管理法 1999)
key_requirements: Deux régimes: 1) Food classique (sans allégation santé) = enregistrement générique aliment non requis mais notification possible; 2) «Health Food» (健康食品) avec allégation santé = enregistrement obligatoire FDA TW; 13 fonctions de santé reconnues; essais cliniques ou GRAS requis; double vignette obligatoire
notes: Taiwan Health Food = pré-approbation + coûts élevés; sans allégation = food normal; vitamines pures = souvent commercialisées comme food sans Health Food status; étiquetage en mandarin

regulation_name: Loi sur la sécurité sanitaire des aliments — Japon
regulation_ref: 食品衛生法 (Food Sanitation Act) + Règlement complémentaire
enforcement_body: Ministère de la Santé MHLW + Consumer Affairs Agency (CAA)
key_requirements: Food Safety Act = base; compléments sans allégation = food général; pas de notification centrale requise pour vitamines pures commercialisées comme food; étiquetage obligatoire: identité; contenu; date; fabricant; restrictions imports (pesticides; additifs; contaminants)
notes: Food Safety Act révisé 2018; importation: contrôle aux frontières; peut nécessiter certificat sanitaire exportateur; additifs alimentaires liste positive MHLW

regulation_ref: 機能性表示食品 (Kinou-sei Hyouji Shokuhin) — régime CAA 2015
key_requirements: Notification CAA 60 jours avant commercialisation pour food with function claim; base scientifique requise (RCT ou SR); allégation structure/fonction sur étiquette; disclaimer obligatoire; «FOSHU» (Foods for Specified Health Uses) = approbation gouvernementale vs FFC = notification
notes: FFC créé 2015: plus de 7000 produits notifiés; permet allégations santé sans approbation préalable mais sur base scientifique solide; CAA peut demander retrait si base insuffisante

regulation_name: Health Functional Food Act — Corée du Sud
regulation_ref: 건강기능식품에 관한 법률 (Health Functional Food Act 2002 + amendments 2022)
enforcement_body: MFDS (Ministry of Food and Drug Safety)
key_requirements: Compléments vitamines/minéraux = «Health Functional Food» (건강기능식품); enregistrement ou conformité aux monographies standards MFDS; 95 fonctionnalités agréées; fabricant/importateur = licence MFDS obligatoire; GMP obligatoires (certification usine); étiquetage en coréen; allégations pré-approuvées seulement
notes: Corée = régime très structuré; monographies MFDS définissent critères pour chaque ingrédient; vitamines = monographies préétablies; importation = importer licence + MFDS product approval; coût élevé

regulation_ref: 건강기능식품 기준 및 규격 (MFDS Notice — Health Functional Food Standards)
key_requirements: Spécifications détaillées pour chaque ingrédient fonctionnel reconnu; paramètres: pureté; LMS; méthodes d'analyse; microbiologie; métaux lourds; formes galéniques autorisées; chaque mise à jour MFDS peut modifier les spécifications
notes: Mise à jour régulière des spécifications; importateur doit suivre les révisions MFDS; certification lot exigée pour chaque importation

regulation_name: Health Sciences Authority + Sale of Food Act (Singapour)
regulation_ref: Sale of Food Act (Cap. 283) + Food Regulations + HSA Health Products Act
enforcement_body: HSA (Health Sciences Authority) + SFA (Singapore Food Agency)
key_requirements: Compléments vitamines = food si pas de claim thérapeutique; SFA régule comme aliment (Food Regulations); pas de pré-approbation obligatoire pour food; si claim thérapeutique = Health Product sous HSA Health Products Act; étiquetage: alimentation + avertissements; composition; lot
notes: Singapour: frontière food/health product cruciale; HSA publie liste des compléments reclassifiés comme health products; certains ingredients botaniques = automatiquement health product (ex. ginkgo)

regulation_name: Food Act 1983 + DCA Malaysian Control Authority
regulation_ref: Food Act 1983 + Food Regulations 1985 + Control of Drugs and Cosmetics Regulations 1984 (CDCR)
key_requirements: Vitamines sans claim = food (Food Act 1983); vitamines avec claim thérapeutique ou santé = «traditional medicine» ou produit pharmaceutique = enregistrement NPRA requis; CDCR applicable si claim; notification MAL/NOT requis; étiquetage Bahasa/Anglais
notes: Malaysia: dual-track food vs traditional medicine; «health supplement» = catégorie intermédiaire NPRA; enregistrement NOT (Notification) process 6-12 mois; halal certification souvent requise commercialement

regulation_name: Notification Ministry of Public Health N°293 — Thaïlande
regulation_ref: Notification of the Ministry of Public Health No. 293 B.E. 2548 (2005) — Food Supplement Products
key_requirements: Compléments alimentaires = catégorie spécifique «food supplement»; notification/enregistrement TFDA avant commercialisation; dossier: composition; fabricant; LMS; étiquetage en thaï; mentions obligatoires; interdiction claims maladies; GMP recommandés
notes: TFDA maintient liste des ingrédients autorisés; procédure notification 3-6 mois; importation: permis importation + TFDA approval; étiquetage thaï obligatoire

enforcement_body: Cục An toàn thực phẩm (Vietnam Food Administration) / MOH
key_requirements: Compléments alimentaires = «thực phẩm bảo vệ sức khỏe» (food protecting health); auto-déclaration (tự công bố) pour produits standard; publication sur portail ou journal officiel + notification VFA; LMS; étiquetage en vietnamien; interdiction claims maladies; GMP recommandés
notes: Décret 15/2018 a simplifié le processus: self-declaration pour la plupart des compléments vitamines; VFA peut contrôler post-marché; importation: agent local + enregistrement importateur

regulation_ref: Peraturan BPOM No. 32 Tahun 2022 (Food Supplement — Suplemen Kesehatan)
key_requirements: Compléments alimentaires = «suplemen kesehatan»; enregistrement BPOM obligatoire avant commercialisation; liste substances actives autorisées; LMS; formes galéniques permises; étiquetage en Bahasa Indonesia; mentions obligatoires; GMP (CPKB) certifié requis; validité 5 ans
notes: BPOM enregistrement: 6-18 mois; certificat GMP BPOM du fabricant étranger requis ou fabrication locale; importation = agent BPOM; halal certification BPJPH souvent requise commercialement

regulation_ref: Administrative Order No. 2014-0030 (Regulations for Health Supplements)
enforcement_body: FDA Philippines (Bureau of Food and Drugs — BFAD/FDA PH)
key_requirements: Compléments alimentaires = «health supplements»; enregistrement CPR (Certificate of Product Registration) FDA PH obligatoire avant vente; dossier: formulation; stabilité; GMP certificate; Label; LMS; étiquetage en anglais; mention «food supplement»; interdiction claims maladies
notes: FDA PH CPR: 6-12 mois; renouvellement tous les 3 ans; importation = agent PH + FDA permit; GMP du fabricant étranger acceptable (si FDA-recognized country)

regulation_ref: FSSAI (Food Safety and Standards — Nutraceuticals) Regulations 2022
enforcement_body: FSSAI (Food Safety and Standards Authority of India)
key_requirements: Nutraceuticals/compléments = réglementés par FSSAI depuis 2022; listes substances autorisées + LMS; enregistrement/licence FSSAI obligatoire; étiquetage en anglais + 1 langue officielle régionale; mention «Dietary Supplement»; disclaimer pas de substitut aux médicaments; GMP selon FS&S Rules 2011
notes: Régime consolidé depuis 2022; antérieurement lacunaire; importation = FSSAI import clearance; certains ingrédients ayurvédiques peuvent relever AYUSH (Ministry of AYUSH) selon formulation

regulation_name: Therapeutic Goods Act 1989 — Complementary Medicine Listed (AUST L)
regulation_ref: Therapeutic Goods Act 1989 + Therapeutic Goods Regulations 1990
key_requirements: Compléments vitamines/minéraux = «Listed Medicine» (AUST L) sous TGA; enregistrement TGA obligatoire via ARTG; évaluation limitée de l'efficacité (claims modérés); sécurité et qualité vérifiées; GMP TGA obligatoire; étiquetage: AUST L numéro; indications permises selon liste TGA; Label Approved Language
notes: Australie = régime therapeutic goods même pour vitamines de base; AUST L = auto-assessment par fabricant + TGA post-market; AUST R = full evaluation; très différent du régime food US/EU

regulation_name: Dietary Supplement Regulations 1985 + Therapeutic Products Act 2023 NZ
regulation_ref: Dietary Supplement Regulations 1985 (SR 1985/208) + Therapeutic Products Act 2023 (NZ)
key_requirements: Régime en transition: actuellement Dietary Supplement Regulations 1985 (notification légère); mais Therapeutic Products Act 2023 crée nouveau cadre (entrée en vigueur progressive 2026-2027); futures exigences: licence + enregistrement produit; GMP; LMS harmonisées avec AU possible
notes: NZ en transition réglementaire majeure; TP Act 2023 = implémentation 2026-2027; surveillance accrue transitoire; importateurs doivent anticiper nouvelles exigences; ANZTPA (joint AU-NZ agency) discutée mais non créée

regulation_name: ASEAN Guideline Health Supplement — cadre régional
regulation_ref: ASEAN Guideline on Health Supplement (AHSG — version 2020)
key_requirements: Guideline non contraignante mais servant de référence pour harmonisation; définit: catégorie health supplement; liste positive vitamines/minéraux; LMS recommandées; GMP; étiquetage harmonisé; influence SG/MY/TH/VN/ID/PH vers convergence réglementaire
notes: AHSG = soft law; chaque pays ASEAN maintient sa propre réglementation; guideline favorise reconnaissance mutuelle progressive; ACCSQ PFPWG coordonne

regulation_ref: GSO 1366:2002 (Food Supplements — Vitamin and Mineral Supplements for Humans) + révisions
enforcement_body: GSO (Gulf Standards Organisation) + SFDA (Saudi) + MOHAP (UAE) + QCDD (Qatar) + NFSA (Bahrain/Kuwait/Oman)
key_requirements: Standard régional GCC pour compléments vitamines/minéraux; liste substances autorisées; LMS; formes galéniques; étiquetage en arabe obligatoire + anglais optionnel; notification/enregistrement selon pays; Saudi SFDA = enregistrement obligatoire; UAE MOHAP = enregistrement obligatoire
